//! Algorithm negotiation and the `SSH_MSG_KEXINIT` message (RFC 4253 §7.1).
2
//!
3
//! We offer a deliberately small, modern set (see the crate plan). Because the only
4
//! cipher we offer is an AEAD (`chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com`), the negotiated MAC is
5
//! always implicit and the offered MAC name-list is a formality.
6
7
use rand_core::RngCore;
8
9
use crate::wire::{Reader, Writer};
10
use crate::{Result, SshError, msg};
11
12
// Supported algorithm names, in preference order.
13
pub const KEX_CURVE25519: &str = "curve25519-sha256";
14
pub const KEX_CURVE25519_LIBSSH: &str = "curve25519-sha256@libssh.org";
15
pub const HOSTKEY_ED25519: &str = "ssh-ed25519";
16
pub const CIPHER_CHACHA20_POLY1305: &str = "chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com";
17
pub const CIPHER_AES256_GCM: &str = "aes256-gcm@openssh.com";
18
pub const COMPRESSION_NONE: &str = "none";
19
/// Delayed zlib: compression engages only after authentication succeeds.
20
pub const COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH: &str = "zlib@openssh.com";
21
22
/// Strict-KEX markers (OpenSSH `kex-strict-*`, the Terrapin mitigation). Advertised in
23
/// the KEX name-list but never selected as an algorithm; strict mode turns on only when
24
/// both sides advertise their respective marker.
25
pub const KEX_STRICT_CLIENT: &str = "kex-strict-c-v00@openssh.com";
26
pub const KEX_STRICT_SERVER: &str = "kex-strict-s-v00@openssh.com";
27
28
const KEX_ALGORITHMS: &[&str] = &[KEX_CURVE25519, KEX_CURVE25519_LIBSSH];
29
const HOSTKEY_ALGORITHMS: &[&str] = &[HOSTKEY_ED25519];
30
const CIPHERS: &[&str] = &[CIPHER_CHACHA20_POLY1305, CIPHER_AES256_GCM];
31
// Offered but unused: an AEAD cipher is always selected, carrying its own integrity.
32
const MACS: &[&str] = &["hmac-sha2-256"];
33
// `none` is listed first, so compression is off unless a peer prefers `zlib@openssh.com`.
34
const COMPRESSIONS: &[&str] = &[COMPRESSION_NONE, COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH];
35
36
/// A parsed/observed `SSH_MSG_KEXINIT`. The `payload` is the full message bytes
37
/// (starting with the message id) because both sides' KEXINIT payloads feed verbatim
38
/// into the exchange hash as `I_C` / `I_S`.
39
#[derive(Debug, Clone)]
40
pub struct KexInit {
41
    pub payload: Vec<u8>,
42
    pub kex: Vec<Box<str>>,
43
    pub host_key: Vec<Box<str>>,
44
    pub cipher_c2s: Vec<Box<str>>,
45
    pub cipher_s2c: Vec<Box<str>>,
46
    pub comp_c2s: Vec<Box<str>>,
47
    pub comp_s2c: Vec<Box<str>>,
48
    pub first_kex_packet_follows: bool,
49
}
50
51
impl KexInit {
52
    /// Build our KEXINIT with a fresh random cookie, advertising the strict-KEX marker
53
    /// for our role, offering the default cipher and compression sets.
54
0
    pub fn ours(rng: &mut impl RngCore, is_server: bool) -> Self {
55
0
        Self::ours_with(rng, is_server, CIPHERS, COMPRESSIONS)
56
0
    }
57
58
    /// Like [`KexInit::ours`] but offering `ciphers` and `compressions` (in preference
59
    /// order) for both directions. Every name must be one this crate implements;
60
    /// negotiation prefers the client's order, so a client uses this to pin selections.
61
353

/// The algorithms agreed by both sides.
139
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
140
pub struct Negotiated {
141
    pub kex: Box<str>,
142
    pub host_key: Box<str>,
143
    pub cipher_c2s: Box<str>,
144
    pub cipher_s2c: Box<str>,
145
    pub comp_c2s: Box<str>,
146
    pub comp_s2c: Box<str>,
147
}
148
149
/// Run RFC 4253 §7.1 negotiation. `client` and `server` are the two KEXINITs; the rule
150
/// is "the first algorithm on the client's list that the server also supports".
151
53
pub fn negotiate(client: &KexInit, server: &KexInit) -> Result<Negotiated> {
152
    Ok(Negotiated {
153
53
        kex: pick(&client.kex, &server.kex, "kex")?,
154
9
        host_key: pick(&client.host_key, &server.host_key, "host key")?,
155
0
        cipher_c2s: pick(&client.cipher_c2s, &server.cipher_c2s, "cipher c2s")?,
156
0
        cipher_s2c: pick(&client.cipher_s2c, &server.cipher_s2c, "cipher s2c")?,
157
0
        comp_c2s: pick(&client.comp_c2s, &server.comp_c2s, "compression c2s")?,
158
0
        comp_s2c: pick(&client.comp_s2c, &server.comp_s2c, "compression s2c")?,
159
    })
160
53
}
161
162
/// The default offered cipher list (preference order).
163
353
pub fn default_ciphers() -> &'static [&'static str] {
164
353
    CIPHERS
165
353
}
166
167
/// The default offered compression list (preference order; `none` first, so compression
168
/// is off unless a peer prefers it).
169
353
pub fn default_compressions() -> &'static [&'static str] {
170
353
    COMPRESSIONS
171
353
}

62

182

#[cfg(test)]
194
mod tests {
195
    use super::*;
196
    use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng;
197
    use rand_core::SeedableRng;
198
199
    fn rng() -> ChaCha8Rng {
200
        ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(7)
201
    }
202
203
    #[test]
204
    fn ours_roundtrips_through_parse() {
205
        let k = KexInit::ours(&mut rng(), false);
206
        let parsed = KexInit::parse(&k.payload).unwrap();
207
        assert_eq!(parsed.kex, k.kex);
208
        assert_eq!(parsed.host_key, k.host_key);
209
        assert_eq!(parsed.cipher_c2s, k.cipher_c2s);
210
        assert!(!parsed.first_kex_packet_follows);
211
    }
212
213
    #[test]
214
    fn negotiation_prefers_client_order() {
215
        let mut client = KexInit::ours(&mut rng(), false);
216
        let mut server = KexInit::ours(&mut rng(), false);
217
        client.kex = vec![KEX_CURVE25519_LIBSSH.into(), KEX_CURVE25519.into()];
218
        server.kex = vec![KEX_CURVE25519.into(), KEX_CURVE25519_LIBSSH.into()];
219
        // Client lists libssh first and the server supports it → libssh wins.
220
        let n = negotiate(&client, &server).unwrap();
221
        assert_eq!(&*n.kex, KEX_CURVE25519_LIBSSH);
222
    }
223
224
    #[test]
225
    fn negotiation_fails_with_no_overlap() {
226
        let mut client = KexInit::ours(&mut rng(), false);
227
        let server = KexInit::ours(&mut rng(), false);
228
        client.kex = vec!["diffie-hellman-group14-sha1".into()];
229
        assert!(matches!(
230
            negotiate(&client, &server),
231
            Err(SshError::NoCommonAlgorithm("kex"))
232
        ));
233
    }
234
235
    #[test]
236
    fn parse_rejects_wrong_message_id() {
237
        let mut bad = KexInit::ours(&mut rng(), false).payload;
238
        bad[0] = msg::NEWKEYS;
239
        assert!(matches!(KexInit::parse(&bad), Err(SshError::Protocol(_))));
240
    }
241
}

Source

//! User authentication protocol (RFC 4252): service request/accept and the `none`,
2
//! `password`, and `publickey` methods. User public keys are `ssh-ed25519`.
3
//!
4
//! Message bodies are built/parsed here; the client and server session state machines
5
//! ([`crate::client`], [`crate::server`]) drive the exchange.
6
7
use ed25519_dalek::{Signer, SigningKey, VerifyingKey};
8
use rand_core::CryptoRngCore;
9
use zeroize::Zeroize;
10
11
use crate::algo::HOSTKEY_ED25519;
12
use crate::wire::{Reader, Writer};
13
use crate::{Result, SshError, msg};
14
15
/// A user password held only as long as it is needed and scrubbed from memory on drop.
16
/// Dereferences to `&str`, so it is used like a string but never lingers in RAM and is
17
/// redacted in `Debug` output.
18
#[derive(Clone, Default)]
19
pub struct Password(String);
20
21
impl Password {
22
0
    pub fn as_str(&self) -> &str {
23
0
        &self.0
24
0
    }
25
}
26
27

28
0
    fn drop(&mut self) {
29
0
        self.0.zeroize();
30
0
    }
31
}
32

0

0

0
    }
38
}

0

61
0
    }
62

63
64
/// The user-authentication service name.
65
pub const SERVICE_USERAUTH: &str = "ssh-userauth";
66
/// The connection-protocol service name (requested after authentication).
67
pub const SERVICE_CONNECTION: &str = "ssh-connection";
68
69
pub const METHOD_NONE: &str = "none";
70
pub const METHOD_PASSWORD: &str = "password";
71
pub const METHOD_PUBLICKEY: &str = "publickey";
72
73

74

75

76
    verifying: VerifyingKey,
77

/// A `publickey` request, with or without a trailing signature.
247

248
0

266

}

268
/// A parsed `SSH_MSG_USERAUTH_REQUEST` (server side).
269
#[derive(Debug)]
270
pub struct AuthRequest {
271
    pub user: Box<str>,
272
    pub service: Box<str>,
273
    pub method: Method,
274
}
275
276
/// The method-specific part of an auth request.
277
#[derive(Debug)]
278
pub enum Method {
279
    None,
280
    Password {
281
        password: Password,
282
    },
283
    PublicKey {
284
        key_algo: Box<str>,
285
        key_blob: Vec<u8>,
286
        /// Present only when the client included a signature (vs. a probe).
287
        signature: Option<Vec<u8>>,
288
    },
289
    Unknown {
290
        name: Box<str>,
291
    },
292
}
293
294
impl AuthRequest {
295
0
    pub fn parse(payload: &[u8]) -> Result<Self> {
296
0
        let mut r = Reader::new(payload);
297
0
        if r.u8()? != msg::USERAUTH_REQUEST {
298
0
            return Err(SshError::Protocol("expected USERAUTH_REQUEST"));
299
0
        }
300
0
        let user = r.utf8()?.into();
301
0
        let service = r.utf8()?.into();
302
0
        let method_name = r.utf8()?;
303
0
        let method = match method_name {
304
0
            METHOD_NONE => Method::None,
305
0
            METHOD_PASSWORD => {
306
0
                let _change = r.boolean()?; // FALSE; password change not supported
307
                Method::Password {
308
0
                    password: Password::from(r.utf8()?),
309
                }
310
            }
311
0
            METHOD_PUBLICKEY => {
312
0
                let has_sig = r.boolean()?;
313
0
                let key_algo = r.utf8()?.into();
314
0
                let key_blob = r.string()?.to_vec();
315
0
                let signature = if has_sig {
316
0
                    Some(r.string()?.to_vec())
317
                } else {
318
0
                    None
319
                };
320
0
                Method::PublicKey {
321
0
                    key_algo,
322
0
                    key_blob,
323
0
                    signature,
324
0
                }
325
            }
326
0
            other => Method::Unknown { name: other.into() },
327
        };
328
0
        Ok(Self {
329
0
            user,
330
0
            service,
331
0
            method,
332
0
        })
333
0
    }
334
}
335
336
#[cfg(test)]
337
mod tests {
338
    use super::*;
339
    use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng;
340
    use rand_core::SeedableRng;
341
342
    #[test]
343
    fn password_request_roundtrip() {
344
        let bytes = password_request("alice", SERVICE_CONNECTION, "hunter2");
345
        let req = AuthRequest::parse(&bytes).unwrap();
346
        assert_eq!(&*req.user, "alice");
347
        assert_eq!(&*req.service, SERVICE_CONNECTION);
348
        match req.method {
349
            Method::Password { password } => assert_eq!(&*password, "hunter2"),
350
            _ => panic!("expected password method"),
351
        }
352
    }
353
354
    #[test]
355
    fn publickey_probe_has_no_signature() {
356
        let kp = UserKeypair::generate(&mut ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(1));
357
        let blob = kp.public().blob();
358
        let bytes = publickey_request("bob", SERVICE_CONNECTION, HOSTKEY_ED25519, &blob, None);
359
        let req = AuthRequest::parse(&bytes).unwrap();
360
        match req.method {
361
            Method::PublicKey { signature, .. } => assert!(signature.is_none()),
362
            _ => panic!("expected publickey method"),
363
        }
364
    }
365
366
    #[test]
367
    fn publickey_signature_roundtrips_and_verifies() {
368
        let kp = UserKeypair::generate(&mut ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(2));
369
        let pubkey = kp.public();
370
        let session_id = [0x5au8; 32];
371
        let blob = pubkey.blob();
372
        let signed = publickey_signed_data(
373
            &session_id,
374
            "bob",
375
            SERVICE_CONNECTION,
376
            HOSTKEY_ED25519,
377
            &blob,
378
        );
379
        let sig = kp.sign(&signed);
380
        assert!(pubkey.verify(&signed, &sig).is_ok());
381
382
        // A tampered signed-data must fail.
383
        let mut bad = signed.clone();
384
        bad[0] ^= 0xff;
385
        assert!(matches!(
386
            pubkey.verify(&bad, &sig),
387
            Err(SshError::AuthFailed)
388
        ));
389
    }
390
}

Count
Source
1
//! Packet ciphers. The active cipher changes at `NEWKEYS`, so the set of ciphers is a
2
//! runtime [`Cipher`] enum (matched, never `dyn`) rather than a generic parameter.
3
//!
4
//! Before key exchange completes, [`Cipher::None`] performs the plaintext framing from
5
//! [`crate::packet`]. After `NEWKEYS`, [`Cipher::ChaCha20Poly1305`] implements
6
//! `chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com` (OpenSSH `PROTOCOL.chacha20poly1305`):
7
//!
8
//! * 64 bytes of key material: `K_2 = key[0..32]` (payload), `K_1 = key[32..64]` (length).
9
//! * The 4-byte packet length is encrypted with `K_1` (ChaCha20-Legacy, counter 0).
10
//! * The Poly1305 key is the first 32 bytes of the `K_2` keystream at counter 0; the
11
//!   payload is encrypted with `K_2` from counter 1 (byte offset 64).
12
//! * The Poly1305 tag authenticates `encrypted_length ‖ encrypted_payload` and is appended.
13
14
use aes_gcm::aead::AeadInPlace;
15
use aes_gcm::{Aes256Gcm, KeyInit as _};
16
use chacha20::ChaCha20Legacy;
17
use chacha20::cipher::{KeyIvInit, StreamCipher, StreamCipherSeek};
18
use poly1305::Poly1305;
19
use poly1305::universal_hash::KeyInit;
20
use rand_core::RngCore;
21
use subtle::ConstantTimeEq;
22
use zeroize::{Zeroize, ZeroizeOnDrop, Zeroizing};
23
24
use crate::algo::{CIPHER_AES256_GCM, CIPHER_CHACHA20_POLY1305};
25
use crate::packet::{self, MAX_PACKET_LENGTH, MIN_PADDING};
26
use crate::{Result, SshError};
27
28
/// Length of the AEAD authentication tag (both ciphers use 16).
29
const TAG_LEN: usize = 16;
30
/// Block-alignment size for chacha20-poly1305's padded region.
31
const CHACHA_BLOCK: usize = 8;
32
/// Block-alignment size for aes256-gcm's padded region.
33
const GCM_BLOCK: usize = 16;
34
35
/// Padding length for an AEAD packet: the 4-byte packet-length field is not part of the
36
/// block-aligned region, so `padding_length ‖ payload ‖ padding` (`packet_length`) must
37
/// be a multiple of `block`, with padding >= 4.
38
0
fn aead_padding_len(payload_len: usize, block: usize) -> usize {
39
0
    let unpadded = 1 + payload_len; // padding_length byte + payload
40
0
    let mut pad = block - (unpadded % block);
41
0
    if pad < MIN_PADDING {
42
0
        pad += block;
43
0
    }
44
0
    pad
45
0
}
46
47
/// An active packet cipher for one direction. The key material is scrubbed from memory
48
/// when the cipher is dropped (at `NEWKEYS`/rekey and when the connection ends).
49
#[derive(Zeroize, ZeroizeOnDrop)]
50
pub enum Cipher {
51
    /// Plaintext framing used before `NEWKEYS`.
52
    None,
53
    /// `chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com`.
54
    ChaCha20Poly1305 { k2: [u8; 32], k1: [u8; 32] },
55
    /// `aes256-gcm@openssh.com`. `iv` holds the current 12-byte nonce whose trailing
56
    /// 8-byte invocation counter increments after each packet (OpenSSH `PROTOCOL`).
57
    Aes256Gcm { key: [u8; 32], iv: [u8; 12] },
58
}
59
60
impl Cipher {
61
    /// Bytes of encryption-key material a named cipher consumes from the KDF.
62
0
    pub fn key_len(name: &str) -> Result<usize> {
63
0
        match name {
64
0
            CIPHER_CHACHA20_POLY1305 => Ok(64),
65
0
            CIPHER_AES256_GCM => Ok(32),
66
0
            _ => Err(SshError::NoCommonAlgorithm("cipher")),
67
        }
68
0
    }
69
70
    /// Bytes of IV material a named cipher consumes from the KDF.
71
0
    pub fn iv_len(name: &str) -> Result<usize> {
72
0
        match name {
73
0
            CIPHER_CHACHA20_POLY1305 => Ok(0),
74
0
            CIPHER_AES256_GCM => Ok(12),
75
0
            _ => Err(SshError::NoCommonAlgorithm("cipher")),
76
        }
77
0
    }
78
79
    /// Construct a cipher from its negotiated name and derived key/IV material.
80
0
    pub fn new(name: &str, key: &[u8], iv: &[u8]) -> Result<Self> {
81
0
        match name {
82
0
            CIPHER_CHACHA20_POLY1305 => {
83
0
                if key.len() != 64 {
84
0
                    return Err(SshError::Kex("chacha20-poly1305 needs 64 key bytes"));
85
0
                }
86
0
                let mut k2 = [0u8; 32];
87
0
                let mut k1 = [0u8; 32];
88
0
                k2.copy_from_slice(&key[0..32]);
89
0
                k1.copy_from_slice(&key[32..64]);
90
0
                Ok(Cipher::ChaCha20Poly1305 { k2, k1 })
91
            }
92
0
            CIPHER_AES256_GCM => {
93
0
                if key.len() != 32 || iv.len() != 12 {
94
0
                    return Err(SshError::Kex("aes256-gcm needs 32 key + 12 IV bytes"));
95
0
                }
96
0
                let mut k = [0u8; 32];
97
0
                let mut v = [0u8; 12];
98
0
                k.copy_from_slice(key);
99
0
                v.copy_from_slice(iv);
100
0
                Ok(Cipher::Aes256Gcm { key: k, iv: v })
101
            }
102
0
            _ => Err(SshError::NoCommonAlgorithm("cipher")),
103
        }
104
0
    }
105
106
    /// Encrypt `payload` into a complete on-wire packet for sequence number `seqnr`.
107
353
    pub fn seal(&mut self, seqnr: u32, payload: &[u8], rng: &mut impl RngCore) -> Vec<u8> {
108
353
        match self {
109
353
            Cipher::None => packet::encode_plain(payload, rng),
110
0
            Cipher::Aes256Gcm { key, iv } => gcm_seal(key, iv, payload, rng),
111
0
            Cipher::ChaCha20Poly1305 { k2, k1 } => {
112
0
                let pad = aead_padding_len(payload.len(), CHACHA_BLOCK);
113
0
                let packet_length = (1 + payload.len() + pad) as u32;
114
115
                // payload region = padding_length ‖ payload ‖ random padding
116
0
                let mut region = Vec::with_capacity(packet_length as usize);
117
0
                region.push(pad as u8);
118
0
                region.extend_from_slice(payload);
119
0
                let pad_start = region.len();
120
0
                region.resize(pad_start + pad, 0);
121
0
                rng.fill_bytes(&mut region[pad_start..]);
122
123
0
                let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(4 + region.len() + TAG_LEN);
124
125
                // Encrypt the 4-byte length with K_1.
126
0
                let mut len_bytes = packet_length.to_be_bytes();
127
0
                length_cipher(k1, seqnr).apply_keystream(&mut len_bytes);
128
0
                out.extend_from_slice(&len_bytes);
129
130
                // Encrypt the payload region with K_2 (counter 1) and derive the poly key.
131
0
                let (poly_key, mut main) = payload_cipher(k2, seqnr);
132
0
                main.apply_keystream(&mut region);
133
0
                out.extend_from_slice(&region);
134
135
0
                let tag = poly1305_tag(&poly_key, &out);
136
0
                out.extend_from_slice(&tag);
137
0
                out
138
            }
139
        }
140
353
    }

142
    /// Try to decrypt one packet from the front of `buf`. The returned plaintext is held
143
    /// in a [`Zeroizing`] buffer so it is scrubbed from memory once dropped.
144
931
    pub fn open(&mut self, seqnr: u32, buf: &[u8]) -> Result<Option<(Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>, usize)>> {
145
931
        match self {
146
            // Pre-NEWKEYS framing carries no secrets, but wrap it for a uniform type.
147
931
            Cipher::None => Ok(packet::decode_plain(buf)?.map(|(p, n)| (Zeroizing::new(p), n))),
148
0
            Cipher::Aes256Gcm { key, iv } => gcm_open(key, iv, buf),
149
0
            Cipher::ChaCha20Poly1305 { k2, k1 } => {
150
0
                if buf.len() < 4 {
151
0
                    return Ok(None);
152
0
                }
153
                // Decrypt the length with K_1 to learn how many bytes to expect.
154
0
                let mut len_bytes = [buf[0], buf[1], buf[2], buf[3]];
155
0
                length_cipher(k1, seqnr).apply_keystream(&mut len_bytes);
156
0
                let packet_length = u32::from_be_bytes(len_bytes) as usize;
157
                // The AEAD region must be a positive multiple of the block size.
158
0
                if packet_length < CHACHA_BLOCK
159
0
                    || !packet_length.is_multiple_of(CHACHA_BLOCK)
160
0
                    || packet_length > MAX_PACKET_LENGTH
161
                {
162
0
                    return Err(SshError::BadPacket("packet_length out of range"));
163
0
                }
164
165
0
                let total = 4 + packet_length + TAG_LEN;
166
0
                if buf.len() < total {
167
0
                    return Ok(None);
168
0
                }
169
170
                // Authenticate encrypted_length ‖ encrypted_payload before decrypting.
171
0
                let (poly_key, mut main) = payload_cipher(k2, seqnr);
172
0
                let tag = poly1305_tag(&poly_key, &buf[..4 + packet_length]);
173
0
                let received = &buf[4 + packet_length..total];
174
0
                if tag.ct_eq(received).unwrap_u8() != 1 {
175
0
                    return Err(SshError::Integrity);
176
0
                }
177
178
                // Decrypt the payload region. Held in `Zeroizing` so the decrypted bytes
179
                // (which may include credentials) are wiped on every exit path.
180
0
                let mut region = Zeroizing::new(buf[4..4 + packet_length].to_vec());
181
0
                main.apply_keystream(region.as_mut_slice());
182
183
0
                let padding_length = region[0] as usize;
184
0
                if padding_length < MIN_PADDING || padding_length + 1 > packet_length {
185
0
                    return Err(SshError::BadPacket("invalid padding_length"));
186
0
                }
187
0
                let payload = region[1..packet_length - padding_length].to_vec();
188
0
                Ok(Some((Zeroizing::new(payload), total)))
189
            }
190
        }
191
931
    }
192
}
193
194
/// ChaCha20-Legacy instance keyed by `K_1` for the length field (counter 0).
195
0
fn length_cipher(k1: &[u8; 32], seqnr: u32) -> ChaCha20Legacy {
196
0
    ChaCha20Legacy::new(k1.into(), &nonce(seqnr).into())
197
0
}
198
199
/// Returns the Poly1305 key (block-0 keystream) and a `K_2` cipher positioned at
200
/// counter 1 (byte offset 64) ready to encrypt/decrypt the payload region. The one-time
201
/// MAC key is held in a [`Zeroizing`] buffer so it is scrubbed from memory on drop.
202
0
fn payload_cipher(k2: &[u8; 32], seqnr: u32) -> (Zeroizing<[u8; 32]>, ChaCha20Legacy) {
203
0
    let mut cipher = ChaCha20Legacy::new(k2.into(), &nonce(seqnr).into());
204
0
    let mut poly_key = Zeroizing::new([0u8; 32]);
205
0
    cipher.apply_keystream(poly_key.as_mut_slice());
206
0
    cipher.seek(64u64);
207
0
    (poly_key, cipher)
208
0
}
209
210
0
fn poly1305_tag(key: &[u8; 32], data: &[u8]) -> [u8; TAG_LEN] {
211
0
    let mac = Poly1305::new(key.into());
212
0
    mac.compute_unpadded(data).into()
213
0
}
214
215
/// The 8-byte nonce: the packet sequence number as a big-endian `uint64`.
216
0
fn nonce(seqnr: u32) -> [u8; 8] {
217
0
    (seqnr as u64).to_be_bytes()
218
0
}
219
220
// --- aes256-gcm@openssh.com (RFC 5647 framing) ---
221
//
222
// The 4-byte packet length is sent in cleartext and authenticated as AAD; the rest
223
// (padding_length ‖ payload ‖ padding) is encrypted. The 12-byte IV's trailing 8 bytes
224
// are an invocation counter incremented after every packet.
225
226
0
fn gcm_seal(key: &[u8; 32], iv: &mut [u8; 12], payload: &[u8], rng: &mut impl RngCore) -> Vec<u8> {
227
0
    let pad = aead_padding_len(payload.len(), GCM_BLOCK);
228
0
    let packet_length = (1 + payload.len() + pad) as u32;
229
230
0
    let mut region = Vec::with_capacity(packet_length as usize);
231
0
    region.push(pad as u8);
232
0
    region.extend_from_slice(payload);
233
0
    let pad_start = region.len();
234
0
    region.resize(pad_start + pad, 0);
235
0
    rng.fill_bytes(&mut region[pad_start..]);
236
237
0
    let aad = packet_length.to_be_bytes();
238
0
    let cipher = Aes256Gcm::new(key.into());
239
0
    let tag = cipher
240
0
        .encrypt_in_place_detached(iv.as_ref().into(), &aad, &mut region)
241
0
        .expect("aes-gcm encryption");
242
243
0
    gcm_increment(iv);
244
245
0
    let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(4 + region.len() + TAG_LEN);
246
0
    out.extend_from_slice(&aad);
247
0
    out.extend_from_slice(&region);
248
0
    out.extend_from_slice(&tag);
249
0
    out
250
0
}

Unexecuted instantiation: _RINvNtCs2RdDlyCxcWB_13ssh_transport6cipher8gcm_sealpEB4_

252
0
fn gcm_open(
253
0
    key: &[u8; 32],
254
0
    iv: &mut [u8; 12],
255
0
    buf: &[u8],
256
0
) -> Result<Option<(Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>, usize)>> {
257
0

258
0
        return Ok(None);
259
0
    }
260
0
    let packet_length = u32::from_be_bytes([buf[0], buf[1], buf[2], buf[3]]) as usize;
261
0
    if packet_length < GCM_BLOCK
262
0
        || !packet_length.is_multiple_of(GCM_BLOCK)
263
0
        || packet_length > MAX_PACKET_LENGTH
264
    {
265
0
        return Err(SshError::BadPacket("packet_length out of range"));
266
0
    }
267
0
    let total = 4 + packet_length + TAG_LEN;
268
0
    if buf.len() < total {
269
0
        return Ok(None);
270
0
    }
271
272
0
    let aad = &buf[0..4];
273
    // Held in `Zeroizing` so the decrypted plaintext is wiped on every exit path.
274
0
    let mut region = Zeroizing::new(buf[4..4 + packet_length].to_vec());
275
0
    let tag = &buf[4 + packet_length..total];
276
277
0
    let cipher = Aes256Gcm::new(key.into());
278
0
    cipher
279
0
        .decrypt_in_place_detached(iv.as_ref().into(), aad, region.as_mut_slice(), tag.into())
280
0
        .map_err(|_| SshError::Integrity)?;
281
282
0

283
284
0
    let padding_length = region[0] as usize;
285
0
    if padding_length < MIN_PADDING || padding_length + 1 > packet_length {
286
0

287
0
    }
288
0
    let payload = region[1..packet_length - padding_length].to_vec();
289
0
    Ok(Some((Zeroizing::new(payload), total)))
290
0
}
291
292
/// Increment the trailing 8-byte big-endian invocation counter of a gcm IV.
293
0
fn gcm_increment(iv: &mut [u8; 12]) {
294
0
    let counter = u64::from_be_bytes(iv[4..12].try_into().unwrap()).wrapping_add(1);
295
0
    iv[4..12].copy_from_slice(&counter.to_be_bytes());
296
0
}
297
298
#[cfg(test)]
299
mod tests {
300
    use super::*;
301
    use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng;
302
    use rand_core::SeedableRng;
303
304
    fn cipher() -> Cipher {
305
        let key: Vec<u8> = (0..64u8).collect();
306
        Cipher::new(CIPHER_CHACHA20_POLY1305, &key, &[]).unwrap()
307
    }
308
309
    fn gcm() -> Cipher {
310
        let key: Vec<u8> = (0..32u8).collect();
311
        let iv: Vec<u8> = (0..12u8).collect();
312
        Cipher::new(CIPHER_AES256_GCM, &key, &iv).unwrap()
313
    }
314
315
    #[test]
316
    fn seal_open_roundtrip() {
317
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(99);
318
        let mut c = cipher();
319
        for (seqnr, payload) in [
320
            (0u32, vec![]),
321
            (1, b"hello world".to_vec()),
322
            (2, vec![0xABu8; 3000]),
323
        ] {
324
            let frame = c.seal(seqnr, &payload, &mut rng);
325
            let (out, consumed) = c.open(seqnr, &frame).unwrap().unwrap();
326
            assert_eq!(*out, payload);
327
            assert_eq!(consumed, frame.len());
328
        }
329
    }
330
331
    #[test]
332
    fn gcm_seal_open_roundtrip() {
333
        // Separate instances (like cipher_out/cipher_in) so their IV counters advance
334
        // in lockstep, matching how a real connection is keyed.
335
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(42);
336
        let mut tx = gcm();
337
        let mut rx = gcm();
338
        for (seqnr, payload) in [
339
            (0u32, vec![]),
340
            (1, b"gcm payload".to_vec()),
341
            (2, vec![7u8; 5000]),
342
        ] {
343
            let frame = tx.seal(seqnr, &payload, &mut rng);
344
            let (out, consumed) = rx.open(seqnr, &frame).unwrap().unwrap();
345
            assert_eq!(*out, payload);
346
            assert_eq!(consumed, frame.len());
347
        }
348
    }
349
350
    #[test]
351
    fn gcm_tampered_fails_integrity() {
352
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(1);
353
        let mut tx = gcm();
354
        let mut rx = gcm();
355
        let mut frame = tx.seal(0, b"important", &mut rng);
356
        let i = frame.len() / 2;
357
        frame[i] ^= 0x01;
358
        assert!(matches!(rx.open(0, &frame), Err(SshError::Integrity)));
359
    }
360
361
    #[test]
362
    fn wrong_sequence_number_is_rejected() {
363
        // Opening with the wrong seqnr corrupts the length decryption (caught as a bad
364
        // packet) and/or fails the MAC — either way it must be rejected.
365
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(7);
366
        let mut c = cipher();
367
        let frame = c.seal(5, b"data", &mut rng);
368
        assert!(c.open(6, &frame).is_err());
369
    }
370
371
    #[test]
372
    fn tampered_ciphertext_fails_integrity() {
373
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(8);
374
        let mut c = cipher();
375
        let mut frame = c.seal(0, b"important", &mut rng);
376
        let i = frame.len() / 2;
377
        frame[i] ^= 0x01;
378
        assert!(matches!(c.open(0, &frame), Err(SshError::Integrity)));
379
    }
380
381
    #[test]
382
    fn open_needs_more_bytes() {
383
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(9);
384
        let mut c = cipher();
385
        let frame = c.seal(0, b"partial", &mut rng);
386
        assert_eq!(c.open(0, &frame[..2]).unwrap(), None);
387
        assert_eq!(c.open(0, &frame[..frame.len() - 1]).unwrap(), None);
388
    }
389
}
/home/nick/proj/rust/ssh-bench/crates/ssh-transport/src/client.rs
Line
Count
Source
1
//! Client-side session: wraps the [`Transport`], enforces host-key policy, drives user
2
//! authentication, and runs a session channel (`exec`). Credentials and known-hosts
3
//! policy come from a [`ClientAuthHandler`] (a generic parameter — no `dyn`).
4
5
use rand_core::{CryptoRng, RngCore};
6
7
use crate::algo::HOSTKEY_ED25519;
8
use crate::auth::{self, Password, UserKeypair};
9
use crate::connection::{self as conn, Channel};
10
use crate::transport::Event;
11
use crate::wire::Reader;
12
use crate::{HostPublicKey, Result, SshError, Transport, msg};
13
14
/// One authentication attempt the client will make.
15
pub enum AuthAttempt {
16
    /// A password (scrubbed from memory on drop).
17
    Password(Password),
18
    /// Boxed because an ed25519 keypair is much larger than the other variant.
19
    PublicKey(Box<UserKeypair>),
20
}
21
22
/// Client policy: host-key trust and credential selection.
23
pub trait ClientAuthHandler {
24
    fn username(&self) -> Box<str>;
25
    /// Decide whether to trust the server's host key (signature already verified).
26
    fn verify_host_key(&mut self, key: &HostPublicKey) -> bool;
27
    /// Next attempt to make; `can_continue` lists methods the server allows. `None`
28
    /// gives up.
29
    fn next_auth(&mut self, can_continue: &[Box<str>]) -> Option<AuthAttempt>;
30
}
31
32
/// High-level client events.
33
#[derive(Debug)]
34
pub enum ClientEvent {
35
    Banner(Box<str>),
36
    Authenticated,
37
    AuthFailed {
38
        methods: Vec<Box<str>>,
39
    },
40
    HostKeyRejected,
41
    /// The session channel was opened and is ready for an `exec`/`shell` request.
42
    ChannelReady {
43
        channel: u32,
44
    },
45
    /// Opening the session channel failed.
46
    ChannelOpenFailure {
47
        reason: u32,
48
        description: Box<str>,
49
    },
50
    /// Process stdout.
51
    Stdout(Vec<u8>),
52
    /// Process stderr.
53
    Stderr(Vec<u8>),
54
    /// The command's exit status.
55
    ExitStatus(u32),
56
    /// The server refused the exec/shell/subsystem request (`CHANNEL_FAILURE`).
57
    RequestFailed,
58
    /// The channel was closed by the server.
59
    ChannelClosed,
60
    Disconnect {
61
        reason: u32,
62
        description: Box<str>,
63
    },
64
}
65
66
#[derive(PartialEq, Eq)]
67
enum State {
68
    Handshaking,
69
    ExpectServiceAccept,
70
    Authenticating,
71
    Authenticated,
72
    Done,
73
}
74
75
const LOCAL_CHANNEL: u32 = 0;
76
77
/// The session request to issue once the channel is confirmed open.
78
enum PendingRequest {
79
    Exec(Box<str>),
80
    Shell,
81
}
82
83
/// A client-side SSH connection (single session channel).
84
pub struct ClientConnection<R: RngCore + CryptoRng, H: ClientAuthHandler> {
85
    transport: Transport<R>,
86
    handler: H,
87
    state: State,
88
    host_rejected: bool,
89
    channel: Option<Channel>,
90
    pending: Option<PendingRequest>,
91
    events: std::collections::VecDeque<ClientEvent>,
92
}
93
94
impl<R: RngCore + CryptoRng, H: ClientAuthHandler> ClientConnection<R, H> {
95
0
    pub fn new(rng: R, handler: H) -> Self {
96
0
        Self::with_transport(Transport::new_client(rng), handler)
97
0
    }
98
99
    /// Like [`ClientConnection::new`] but offering `ciphers` (in preference order). Since
100
    /// negotiation prefers the client's order, this pins which cipher is selected when
101
    /// the server supports it — useful for testing a specific suite.
102
0
    pub fn with_cipher_preference(rng: R, handler: H, ciphers: &[&str]) -> Self {
103
0
        Self::with_transport(Transport::new_client_with_ciphers(rng, ciphers), handler)
104
0
    }
105
106
    /// Like [`ClientConnection::new`] but offering `compression` (in preference order).
107
    /// Listing `zlib@openssh.com` first turns on delayed compression when the server
108
    /// supports it (compression engages only after authentication).
109
0
    pub fn with_compression_preference(rng: R, handler: H, compression: &[&str]) -> Self {
110
0
        Self::with_transport(
111
0
            Transport::new_client_with_compression(rng, compression),
112
0
            handler,
113
        )
114
0
    }
115
116
0
    fn with_transport(transport: Transport<R>, handler: H) -> Self {
117
0
        Self {
118
0
            transport,
119
0
            handler,
120
0
            state: State::Handshaking,
121
0
            host_rejected: false,
122
0
            channel: None,
123
0
            pending: None,
124
0
            events: std::collections::VecDeque::new(),
125
0
        }
126
0
    }
127
128
    /// The cipher negotiated for this connection, once the handshake has progressed far
129
    /// enough (e.g. `chacha20-poly1305@openssh.com`).
130
0
    pub fn negotiated_cipher(&self) -> Option<&str> {
131
0
        self.transport.negotiated_cipher()
132
0
    }
133
134
    /// The compression negotiated for this connection (e.g. `none` or `zlib@openssh.com`).
135
0
    pub fn negotiated_compression(&self) -> Option<&str> {
136
0
        self.transport.negotiated_compression()
137
0
    }
138
139
    /// Whether delayed compression has engaged (after authentication).
140
0
    pub fn is_compression_active(&self) -> bool {
141
0
        self.transport.is_compression_active()
142
0
    }
143
144
0
    pub fn on_input(&mut self, data: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
145
0
        self.transport.on_input(data)?;
146
0
        self.pump()
147
0
    }
148
149
0
    pub fn take_output(&mut self) -> Vec<u8> {
150
0
        self.transport.take_output()
151
0
    }
152
153
0
    pub fn poll_event(&mut self) -> Option<ClientEvent> {
154
0
        self.events.pop_front()
155
0
    }
156
157
0
    pub fn is_authenticated(&self) -> bool {
158
0
        matches!(self.state, State::Authenticated)
159
0
    }
160
161
    /// Whether we have queued our own disconnect; the driver should flush and close.
162
0
    pub fn is_closing(&self) -> bool {
163
0
        self.transport.is_closing()
164
0
    }
165
166
0
    pub fn session_id(&self) -> Option<&[u8]> {
167
0
        self.transport.session_id()
168
0
    }
169
170
    /// Begin a key re-exchange (queues application traffic until it completes).
171
0
    pub fn initiate_rekey(&mut self) {
172
0
        self.transport.initiate_rekey();
173
0
    }
174
175
    /// Tune the re-key flood guard: how many server-initiated re-keys are tolerated with
176
    /// no application traffic in between before the connection is dropped.
177
0
    pub fn set_max_consecutive_rekeys(&mut self, n: u32) {
178
0
        self.transport.set_max_consecutive_peer_rekeys(n);
179
0
    }
180
181
    /// Open a session channel and run `command` once the channel is confirmed. Must be
182
    /// called after [`ClientEvent::Authenticated`].
183
0
    pub fn exec(&mut self, command: &str) -> Result<()> {
184
0
        self.open_session(PendingRequest::Exec(command.into()))
185
0
    }
186
187
    /// Open a session channel and request an interactive shell once confirmed.
188
0
    pub fn shell(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
189
0
        self.open_session(PendingRequest::Shell)
190
0
    }
191
192
0
    fn open_session(&mut self, request: PendingRequest) -> Result<()> {
193
0
        if !self.is_authenticated() {
194
0
            return Err(SshError::Protocol("session open before authentication"));
195
0
        }
196
0
        self.channel = Some(Channel::new(LOCAL_CHANNEL));
197
0
        self.pending = Some(request);
198
0
        self.transport
199
0
            .send_packet(&conn::channel_open_session(LOCAL_CHANNEL))
200
0
    }
201
202
    /// Send process stdin (respecting the remote window).
203
0
    pub fn write_stdin(&mut self, data: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
204
0
        if let Some(ch) = self.channel.as_mut() {
205
0
            ch.enqueue_stdout(data);
206
0
        }
207
0
        self.flush_channel()
208
0
    }
209
210
    /// Signal end-of-input on stdin. EOF is deferred until any queued stdin has been
211
    /// flushed, so it never overtakes buffered data when the window opens late.
212
0
    pub fn send_eof(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
213
0
        if let Some(ch) = self.channel.as_mut() {
214
0
            ch.request_eof();
215
0
        }
216
0
        self.flush_channel()
217
0
    }
218
219
0
    fn pump(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
220
0
        while let Some(event) = self.transport.poll_event() {
221
0
            match event {
222
0
                Event::ServerHostKey(key) => {
223
0
                    if !self.handler.verify_host_key(&key) {
224
0
                        self.host_rejected = true;
225
0
                        self.events.push_back(ClientEvent::HostKeyRejected);
226
0
                        self.transport
227
0
                            .disconnect(msg::disconnect::KEY_EXCHANGE_FAILED, "host key rejected");
228
0
                        self.state = State::Done;
229
0
                    }
230
                }
231
                Event::Established => {
232
0
                    if !self.host_rejected {
233
0
                        self.transport
234
0
                            .send_packet(&auth::service_request(auth::SERVICE_USERAUTH))?;
235
0
                        self.state = State::ExpectServiceAccept;
236
0
                    }
237
                }
238
                Event::Disconnect {
239
0
                    reason,
240
0
                    description,
241
0
                } => {

0
                    self.events.push_back(ClientEvent::Disconnect {
243
0
                        reason,
244
0
                        description,
245
0
                    });
246
0
                }
247
0
                Event::Packet(payload) => self.handle_packet(&payload)?,
248
            }
249
        }
250
0
        Ok(())
251
0
    }
252
253
0
    fn handle_packet(&mut self, payload: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
254
0
        match payload.first().copied() {
255
0
            Some(msg::SERVICE_ACCEPT) if self.state == State::ExpectServiceAccept => {
256
0
                self.state = State::Authenticating;
257
0
                self.try_next_auth(&[])
258
            }
259
            Some(msg::USERAUTH_BANNER) => {
260
0
                let mut r = Reader::new(payload);
261
0
                r.u8()?;
262
0
                self.events
263
0
                    .push_back(ClientEvent::Banner(r.utf8().unwrap_or("").into()));
264
0
                Ok(())
265
            }
266
            Some(msg::USERAUTH_SUCCESS) => {
267
0
                self.state = State::Authenticated;
268
0
                self.events.push_back(ClientEvent::Authenticated);
269
0
                Ok(())
270
            }
271
            Some(msg::USERAUTH_FAILURE) => {
272
0
                let mut r = Reader::new(payload);
273
0
                r.u8()?;
274
0
                let methods = r.name_list()?;
275
0
                let _partial = r.boolean()?;
276
0
                self.try_next_auth(&methods)
277
            }
278
0
            Some(msg::USERAUTH_PK_OK) => Ok(()),
279
0
            Some(msg::CHANNEL_OPEN_CONFIRMATION) => self.handle_open_confirmation(payload),
280
0
            Some(msg::CHANNEL_OPEN_FAILURE) => self.handle_open_failure(payload),
281
            // The exec/shell/subsystem request was accepted; nothing to do.
282
0
            Some(msg::CHANNEL_SUCCESS) => Ok(()),
283
            // The request was refused — report it and tear the channel down.
284
0
            Some(msg::CHANNEL_FAILURE) => self.handle_request_failure(),
285
0
            Some(msg::CHANNEL_DATA) => self.handle_channel_data(payload),
286
0
            Some(msg::CHANNEL_EXTENDED_DATA) => self.handle_extended_data(payload),
287
0
            Some(msg::CHANNEL_WINDOW_ADJUST) => self.handle_window_adjust(payload),
288
0
            Some(msg::CHANNEL_REQUEST) => self.handle_channel_request(payload),
289
0
            Some(msg::CHANNEL_EOF) => Ok(()),
290
0
            Some(msg::CHANNEL_CLOSE) => self.handle_channel_close(),
291
0
            _ => Ok(()),
292
        }
293
0
    }
294
295
0
    fn try_next_auth(&mut self, can_continue: &[Box<str>]) -> Result<()> {
296
0
        let user = self.handler.username();
297
0
        match self.handler.next_auth(can_continue) {
298
0
            Some(AuthAttempt::Password(password)) => {
299
0
                let req = auth::password_request(&user, auth::SERVICE_CONNECTION, &password);
300
0
                self.transport.send_packet(&req)
301

302
0
            Some(AuthAttempt::PublicKey(keypair)) => {
303
0
                let session_id = self
304
0
                    .transport
305
0
                    .session_id()
306
0
                    .ok_or(SshError::Protocol("no session id"))?
307
0
                    .to_vec();
308
0
                let blob = keypair.public().blob();
309
0
                let signed = auth::publickey_signed_data(
310
0
                    &session_id,
311
0
                    &user,
312
0
                    auth::SERVICE_CONNECTION,
313
0
                    HOSTKEY_ED25519,
314
0
                    &blob,
315
                );
316
0
                let signature = keypair.sign(&signed);
317
0
                let req = auth::publickey_request(
318
0
                    &user,
319
0
                    auth::SERVICE_CONNECTION,
320
0
                    HOSTKEY_ED25519,
321
0
                    &blob,
322
0
                    Some(&signature),
323
                );
324
0
                self.transport.send_packet(&req)
325
            }
326
            None => {
327
0
                self.events.push_back(ClientEvent::AuthFailed {
328
0
                    methods: can_continue.to_vec(),
329
0
                });
330
0
                self.state = State::Done;
331
0
                Ok(())
332
            }
333
        }
334
0
    }
335
336
0
    fn handle_open_confirmation(&mut self, payload: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
337
0
        let mut r = Reader::new(payload);
338
0
        r.u8()?;
339
0
        let _recipient = r.u32()?;
340
0
        let sender = r.u32()?;
341
0
        let window = r.u32()?;
342
0
        let max_packet = r.u32()?;
343
0
        let Some(ch) = self.channel.as_mut() else {
344
0
            return Ok(());
345

346
0
        ch.set_remote(sender, window, max_packet);
347
0
        let remote = ch.remote_id;
348
0
        self.events.push_back(ClientEvent::ChannelReady {
349
0
            channel: LOCAL_CHANNEL,
350
0
        });
351
0
        match self.pending.take() {
352
0
            Some(PendingRequest::Exec(command)) => self
353
0
                .transport
354
0
                .send_packet(&conn::channel_request_exec(remote, true, &command))?,
355
0
            Some(PendingRequest::Shell) => self
356
0
                .transport
357
0
                .send_packet(&conn::channel_request_shell(remote, true))?,
358
0
            None => {}
359
        }
360
        // Flush any stdin that was buffered before the window was known.
361
0
        self.flush_channel()
362
0
    }
363
364
0
    fn handle_open_failure(&mut self, payload: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {

0
        let mut r = Reader::new(payload);

0
        r.u8()?;
367
0

368

369
0

370
0

371
0

372

            reason,
373
0
            description,
374
0
        });
375
0

376
0
    }
377
378
0
    fn handle_channel_data(&mut self, payload: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {

0

381
0
        let _recipient = r.u32()?;
382
0

383
0

384

385
0
        Ok(())
386
0
    }
387
388
0

0

393
0

394
0

395
0
        self.events.push_back(ClientEvent::Stderr(data));
396
0
        Ok(())
397
0
    }
398
399
0
    fn handle_window_adjust(&mut self, payload: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {

0

0

402
0
        let _recipient = r.u32()?;
403
0
        let add = r.u32()?;
404
0
        if let Some(ch) = self.channel.as_mut() {
405
0
            ch.add_remote_window(add);
406
0
        }
407
0
        self.flush_channel()
408
0
    }
409
410
0
    fn handle_channel_request(&mut self, payload: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
411
0
        let mut r = Reader::new(payload);
412
0
        r.u8()?;
413
0
        let _recipient = r.u32()?;
414
0
        let request = r.utf8()?;
415
0
        let _want_reply = r.boolean()?;
416
0

417
0
            let status = r.u32()?;
418
0
            self.events.push_back(ClientEvent::ExitStatus(status));
419
0
        }
420
0
        Ok(())
421
0
    }
422
423
0
    fn handle_request_failure(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
424
0
        self.events.push_back(ClientEvent::RequestFailed);
425
0
        if let Some(ch) = self.channel.as_mut()
426
0
            && !ch.sent_close
427
        {
428
0
            ch.sent_close = true;
429
0
            let remote = ch.remote_id;
430
0
            self.transport.send_packet(&conn::channel_close(remote))?;
431
0
        }
432
0
        self.channel = None;
433
0
        self.events.push_back(ClientEvent::ChannelClosed);
434
0
        Ok(())
435
0
    }
436
437
0
    fn handle_channel_close(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
438
0
        if let Some(ch) = self.channel.as_mut()
439
0
            && !ch.sent_close
440
        {
441
0
            ch.sent_close = true;
442
0
            let remote = ch.remote_id;
443
0
            self.transport.send_packet(&conn::channel_close(remote))?;
444
0
        }
445
0
        self.channel = None;
446
0
        self.events.push_back(ClientEvent::ChannelClosed);
447
0

448
0
    }
449
450
0
    fn replenish_window(&mut self, len: u32) -> Result<()> {
451
0
        let outcome = self
452
0
            .channel
453
0
            .as_mut()
454
0
            .map(|ch| (ch.remote_id, ch.consume_incoming(len)));
455
0
        match outcome {
456
0
            Some((remote, conn::WindowUpdate::Ok(Some(add)))) => {
457
0
                self.transport
458
0
                    .send_packet(&conn::channel_window_adjust(remote, add))?;
459
            }
460
0
            Some((_, conn::WindowUpdate::Exceeded)) => {
461
0
                // The server overran the window it was granted: drop the connection.
462
0
                self.transport
463
0
                    .disconnect(msg::disconnect::PROTOCOL_ERROR, "channel window exceeded");
464
0
            }
465
0
            _ => {}
466
        }
467
0
        Ok(())
468
0
    }
469
470
0
    fn flush_channel(&mut self) -> Result<()> {
471
0
        let Some(ch) = self.channel.as_mut() else {
472
0
            return Ok(());
473
        };
474
0
        let mut packets = Vec::new();
475
0
        ch.drain_output(|p| packets.push(p));
476
477
0
        let mut eof = None;
478
0

479
0
            ch.sent_eof = true;
480
0
            eof = Some(ch.remote_id);
481
0
        }
482
483
0
        for p in packets {
484
0
            self.transport.send_packet(&p)?;
485
        }
486
0
        if let Some(remote) = eof {
487
0
            self.transport.send_packet(&conn::channel_eof(remote))?;
488
0
        }
489
0
        Ok(())
490
0
    }
491
}

Source
1
//! Payload compression (RFC 4253 §6.2). Only `zlib@openssh.com` is implemented: a
2
//! continuous zlib (RFC 1950) stream whose history carries across packets, with a sync
3
//! flush after each packet so the receiver can recover it immediately. The context is
4
//! reset on every key exchange (handled by the transport).
5
//!
6
//! `zlib@openssh.com` is *delayed*: compression engages only once authentication has
7
//! succeeded, so the auth exchange itself is never compressed (avoiding CRIME-style
8
//! length leaks). The activation point is driven by the transport.
9
//!
10
//! Decompression output is bounded to [`MAX_PACKET_LENGTH`] so a small compressed payload
11
//! cannot expand into a memory-exhausting "decompression bomb".
12
13
use flate2::{Compress, Compression, Decompress, FlushCompress, FlushDecompress, Status};
14
15
use crate::algo::COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH;
16
use crate::packet::MAX_PACKET_LENGTH;
17
use crate::{Result, SshError};
18
19
/// Outbound compression state for one direction.
20
pub enum Compressor {
21
    /// No compression: payloads pass through unchanged.
22
    None,
23
    /// `zlib@openssh.com`: a continuous zlib deflate stream.
24
    Zlib(Box<Compress>),
25
}
26
27
/// Inbound decompression state for one direction.
28
pub enum Decompressor {
29
    None,
30
    Zlib(Box<Decompress>),
31
}
32
33
impl Compressor {
34
    /// Build from a negotiated compression name. Unknown names fall back to `None`.
35
0
    pub fn new(name: &str) -> Self {
36
0
        match name {
37
0
            COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH => {
38
0
                Compressor::Zlib(Box::new(Compress::new(Compression::default(), true)))
39
            }
40
0
            _ => Compressor::None,
41
        }
42
0
    }
43
44
    /// Compress one packet payload, flushing so the peer can decompress it immediately.
45
0
    pub fn compress(&mut self, payload: &[u8]) -> Vec<u8> {
46
0
        match self {
47
0
            Compressor::None => payload.to_vec(),
48
0
            Compressor::Zlib(c) => {
49
0
                let start_in = c.total_in();
50
0
                let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(64 + payload.len());
51
                loop {
52
0
                    if out.len() == out.capacity() {
53
0
                        out.reserve(out.capacity().max(128));
54
0
                    }
55
0
                    let cap = out.capacity();
56
0
                    let in_before = c.total_in();
57
0
                    let out_before = c.total_out();
58
0
                    let consumed = (in_before - start_in) as usize;
59
                    // `compress_vec` writes into the Vec's spare capacity (ensured above)
60
                    // and never fails for in-memory buffers.
61
0
                    let _ = c
62
0
                        .compress_vec(&payload[consumed..], &mut out, FlushCompress::Sync)
63
0
                        .expect("zlib compress");
64
0
                    let consumed_all = (c.total_in() - start_in) as usize == payload.len();
65
0
                    let no_progress = c.total_in() == in_before && c.total_out() == out_before;
66
                    // A sync flush is complete once deflate leaves spare output room (it
67
                    // did not fill the whole buffer); calling again would emit a *new*
68
                    // redundant sync marker, so we must stop here. `no_progress` guards
69
                    // against any pathological stall.
70
0
                    if (consumed_all && out.len() < cap) || no_progress {
71
0
                        break;
72
0
                    }
73
                }
74
0
                out
75
            }
76
        }
77
0
    }
78
}
79
80
impl Decompressor {
81
    /// Build from a negotiated compression name. Unknown names fall back to `None`.
82
0
    pub fn new(name: &str) -> Self {
83
0
        match name {
84
0
            COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH => Decompressor::Zlib(Box::new(Decompress::new(true))),
85
0
            _ => Decompressor::None,
86
        }
87
0
    }
88
89
    /// Decompress one packet payload, bounding the output to [`MAX_PACKET_LENGTH`].
90
0
    pub fn decompress(&mut self, payload: &[u8]) -> Result<Vec<u8>> {
91
0
        match self {
92
0
            Decompressor::None => Ok(payload.to_vec()),
93
0
            Decompressor::Zlib(d) => {
94
0
                let start_in = d.total_in();
95
0
                let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(64 + payload.len() * 2);
96
                loop {
97
0
                    if out.len() == out.capacity() {
98
0
                        out.reserve(out.capacity().max(64));
99
0
                    }
100
0
                    let out_before = d.total_out();
101
0
                    let consumed = (d.total_in() - start_in) as usize;
102
0
                    let status = d
103
0
                        .decompress_vec(&payload[consumed..], &mut out, FlushDecompress::Sync)
104
0
                        .map_err(|_| SshError::Compression("malformed compressed payload"))?;
105
0
                    if out.len() > MAX_PACKET_LENGTH {
106
0
                        return Err(SshError::Compression("decompressed payload too large"));
107
0
                    }
108
0
                    let consumed_all = (d.total_in() - start_in) as usize == payload.len();
109
0
                    let produced = d.total_out() - out_before;
110
0
                    if status == Status::StreamEnd || (consumed_all && produced == 0) {
111
0
                        break;
112
0
                    }
113
                }
114
0
                Ok(out)
115
            }
116
        }
117
0
    }
118
}
119
120
#[cfg(test)]
121
mod tests {
122
    use super::*;
123
    use crate::algo::COMPRESSION_NONE;
124
125
    #[test]
126
    fn roundtrip_across_packets_with_shared_history() {
127
        let mut c = Compressor::new(COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH);
128
        let mut d = Decompressor::new(COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH);
129
        // The stream history persists across packets, so repeated content in a later
130
        // packet should still decompress correctly.
131
        for payload in [
132
            b"GET /index.html HTTP/1.1\r\n".to_vec(),
133
            b"GET /index.html HTTP/1.1\r\n".to_vec(),
134
            vec![0xABu8; 4096],
135
            b"".to_vec(),
136
            b"final".to_vec(),
137
        ] {
138
            let comp = c.compress(&payload);
139
            let back = d.decompress(&comp).unwrap();
140
            assert_eq!(back, payload);
141
        }
142
    }
143
144
    #[test]
145
    fn compresses_repetitive_data() {
146
        let mut c = Compressor::new(COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH);
147
        let payload = vec![0x5Au8; 8192];
148
        let comp = c.compress(&payload);
149
        assert!(comp.len() < payload.len() / 4, "repetitive data should shrink");
150
    }
151
152
    #[test]
153
    fn none_is_passthrough() {
154
        let mut c = Compressor::new(COMPRESSION_NONE);
155
        let mut d = Decompressor::new(COMPRESSION_NONE);
156
        let payload = b"unchanged".to_vec();
157
        assert_eq!(c.compress(&payload), payload);
158
        assert_eq!(d.decompress(&payload).unwrap(), payload);
159
    }
160
161
    #[test]
162
    fn rejects_garbage_compressed_input() {
163
        let mut d = Decompressor::new(COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH);
164
        assert!(matches!(
165
            d.decompress(&[0xff, 0x00, 0x13, 0x37, 0x42]),
166
            Err(SshError::Compression(_))
167
        ));
168
    }
169
170
    #[test]
171
    fn decompression_bomb_is_rejected() {
172
        // A few KiB of compressed data that expands to many MiB is the classic zip-bomb.
173
        // Decompression must refuse it once the output passes MAX_PACKET_LENGTH instead of
174
        // allocating without bound.
175
        let mut c = Compressor::new(COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH);
176
        let huge = vec![0u8; MAX_PACKET_LENGTH * 4]; // 4 MiB of zeros
177
        let bomb = c.compress(&huge);
178
        assert!(
179
            bomb.len() < 16 * 1024,
180
            "bomb should be tiny relative to its expansion ({} bytes)",
181
            bomb.len()
182
        );
183
184
        let mut d = Decompressor::new(COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH);
185
        match d.decompress(&bomb) {
186
            Err(SshError::Compression(_)) => {}
187
            other => panic!("oversized decompression must be rejected, got {other:?}"),
188
        }
189
    }
190
}
/home/nick/proj/rust/ssh-bench/crates/ssh-transport/src/connection.rs
Line
Count
Source
1
//! Connection protocol (RFC 4254): session channels, flow-control windows, and the
2
//! `exec`/`shell` requests. This module holds the wire builders and a [`Channel`]
3
//! bookkeeping type; the client/server sessions drive the state.
4
//!
5
//! Process spawning for `exec`/`shell` is **not** here — it is I/O and belongs to the
6
//! Infra layer. The sessions surface request events and accept output via methods.
7
8
use std::collections::VecDeque;
9
10
use crate::msg;
11
use crate::wire::Writer;
12
13
/// Initial flow-control window we advertise per channel (bytes).
14
pub const DEFAULT_WINDOW: u32 = 1024 * 1024;
15
/// Maximum SSH payload we accept per channel data message.
16
pub const MAX_PACKET: u32 = 32768;
17
/// The only channel type we support.
18
pub const CHANNEL_SESSION: &str = "session";
19
20
/// `CHANNEL_OPEN_FAILURE` reason codes (RFC 4254 §5.1).
21
pub mod open_failure {
22
    pub const ADMINISTRATIVELY_PROHIBITED: u32 = 1;
23
    pub const UNKNOWN_CHANNEL_TYPE: u32 = 3;
24
}
25
26
/// One outgoing chunk awaiting flow-control window: normal stdout or stderr.
27
enum OutItem {
28
    Data(Vec<u8>),
29
    Ext(Vec<u8>),
30
}
31
32
/// Per-channel state and flow-control bookkeeping.
33
pub struct Channel {
34
    pub local_id: u32,
35
    pub remote_id: u32,
36
    /// Bytes the peer may still send us before we must replenish via WINDOW_ADJUST.
37
    local_window: u32,
38
    /// Bytes we may still send the peer.
39
    remote_window: u32,
40
    remote_max_packet: u32,
41
    out: VecDeque<OutItem>,
42
    pub sent_eof: bool,
43
    pub sent_close: bool,
44
    pub recv_close: bool,
45
    pub exit_status: Option<u32>,
46
    want_close: bool,
47
    want_eof: bool,
48
}
49
50
impl Channel {
51
    /// A locally-initiated channel (client opening a session).
52
0
    pub fn new(local_id: u32) -> Self {
53
0
        Self {
54
0
            local_id,
55
0
            remote_id: 0,
56
0
            local_window: DEFAULT_WINDOW,
57
0
            remote_window: 0,
58
0
            remote_max_packet: 0,
59
0
            out: VecDeque::new(),
60
0
            sent_eof: false,
61
0
            sent_close: false,
62
0
            recv_close: false,
63
0
            exit_status: None,
64
0
            want_close: false,
65
0
            want_eof: false,
66
0
        }
67
0
    }
68
69
    /// Request that the channel be closed once all queued output has drained.
70
0
    pub fn request_close(&mut self) {
71
0
        self.want_close = true;
72
0
    }
73
74
0
    pub fn close_requested(&self) -> bool {
75
0
        self.want_close
76
0
    }
77
78
    /// Request that EOF be sent once all queued output has drained (so buffered data is
79
    /// never reordered behind the EOF when the window opens late).
80
0
    pub fn request_eof(&mut self) {
81
0
        self.want_eof = true;
82
0
    }
83
84
0
    pub fn eof_requested(&self) -> bool {
85
0
        self.want_eof
86
0
    }
87
88
    /// Record the peer's parameters once the channel is open.
89
0
    pub fn set_remote(&mut self, remote_id: u32, remote_window: u32, remote_max_packet: u32) {
90
0
        self.remote_id = remote_id;
91
0
        self.remote_window = remote_window;
92
0
        self.remote_max_packet = remote_max_packet.max(1);
93
0
    }
94
95
0
    pub fn enqueue_stdout(&mut self, data: &[u8]) {
96
0
        if !data.is_empty() {
97
0
            self.out.push_back(OutItem::Data(data.to_vec()));
98
0
        }
99
0
    }
100
101
0
    pub fn enqueue_stderr(&mut self, data: &[u8]) {
102
0
        if !data.is_empty() {
103
0
            self.out.push_back(OutItem::Ext(data.to_vec()));
104
0
        }
105
0
    }
106
107
0
    pub fn add_remote_window(&mut self, n: u32) {
108
0
        self.remote_window = self.remote_window.saturating_add(n);
109
0
    }
110
111
    /// Whether all queued output has been flushed.
112
0
    pub fn out_is_empty(&self) -> bool {
113
0
        self.out.is_empty()
114
0
    }
115
116
    /// Emit as many queued data messages as the remote window and max-packet allow,
117
    /// appending them via `emit`. Each call to `emit` receives a ready-to-send payload.
118
0
    pub fn drain_output(&mut self, mut emit: impl FnMut(Vec<u8>)) {
119
0
        while let Some(front) = self.out.front_mut() {
120
0
            let limit = self.remote_window.min(self.remote_max_packet) as usize;
121
0
            if limit == 0 {
122
0
                break;
123
0
            }
124
0
            let (is_ext, buf) = match front {
125
0
                OutItem::Data(b) => (false, b),
126
0
                OutItem::Ext(b) => (true, b),
127
            };
128
0
            let take = buf.len().min(limit);
129
0
            let chunk: Vec<u8> = buf.drain(..take).collect();
130
0
            if buf.is_empty() {
131
0
                self.out.pop_front();
132
0
            }
133
0
            self.remote_window -= take as u32;
134
0
            if is_ext {
135
0
                emit(channel_extended_data(
136
0
                    self.remote_id,
137
0
                    msg::extended_data::STDERR,
138
0
                    &chunk,
139
0
                ));
140
0
            } else {
141
0
                emit(channel_data(self.remote_id, &chunk));
142
0
            }
143
        }
144
0
    }
Unexecuted instantiation: _RINvMNtCs2RdDlyCxcWB_13ssh_transport10connectionNtB3_7Channel12drain_outputNCNvMNtB5_6serverINtB1g_16ServerConnectionNtNtCs66odKybpe6I_11rand_chacha6chacha10ChaCha8RngNtCs2JE6u25WrrW_15server_on_input10NullServerE13flush_channel0EB2H_
Unexecuted instantiation: _RINvMNtCs2RdDlyCxcWB_13ssh_transport10connectionNtB3_7Channel12drain_outputpEB5_
145
146
    /// Account for `len` bytes received from the peer against the window we granted.
147
    /// Returns [`WindowUpdate::Exceeded`] if the peer sent more than its window allowed
148
    /// (a flow-control violation the caller must treat as fatal), otherwise an optional
149
    /// `bytes_to_add` to send in a `WINDOW_ADJUST` once the window should be replenished.
150
0
    pub fn consume_incoming(&mut self, len: u32) -> WindowUpdate {
151
0
        if len > self.local_window {
152
0
            return WindowUpdate::Exceeded;
153
0
        }
154
0
        self.local_window -= len;
155
0
        if self.local_window < DEFAULT_WINDOW / 2 {
156
0
            let add = DEFAULT_WINDOW - self.local_window;
157
0
            self.local_window = DEFAULT_WINDOW;
158
0
            WindowUpdate::Ok(Some(add))
159
        } else {
160
0
            WindowUpdate::Ok(None)
161
        }
162
0
    }
163
}
164
165
/// Outcome of accounting for incoming channel data against the local window.
166
#[derive(Debug, PartialEq, Eq)]
167
pub enum WindowUpdate {
168
    /// Within the granted window; send a `WINDOW_ADJUST` of this many bytes if `Some`.
169
    Ok(Option<u32>),
170
    /// The peer exceeded the window it was granted — a flow-control violation.
171
    Exceeded,
172
}
173
174
// --- message builders (recipient = the peer's channel id) ---
175
176
0
pub fn channel_open_session(sender_channel: u32) -> Vec<u8> {
177
0
    let mut w = Writer::new();
178
0
    w.u8(msg::CHANNEL_OPEN);
179
0
    w.string(CHANNEL_SESSION.as_bytes());
180
0
    w.u32(sender_channel);
181
0
    w.u32(DEFAULT_WINDOW);
182
0
    w.u32(MAX_PACKET);
183
0
    w.into_bytes()
184
0
}
185
186
0

194
0

288
#[cfg(test)]
289
mod tests {
290
    use super::*;
291
292
    #[test]
293
    fn output_respects_window_and_max_packet() {
294
        let mut ch = Channel::new(0);
295
        ch.set_remote(1, 10, 4); // remote window 10, max packet 4
296
        ch.enqueue_stdout(&[0u8; 25]);
297
298
        let mut sent = Vec::new();
299
        ch.drain_output(|p| sent.push(p));
300
        // Window 10, max packet 4 → chunks of 4,4,2 = 10 bytes total, then stops.
301
        let total: usize = sent.iter().map(|p| payload_len(p)).sum();
302
        assert_eq!(total, 10);
303
        assert_eq!(sent.len(), 3);
304
        assert!(!ch.out_is_empty(), "15 bytes remain queued");
305
306
        // Replenish window; the rest flushes.
307
        ch.add_remote_window(100);
308
        let mut more = Vec::new();
309
        ch.drain_output(|p| more.push(p));
310
        let total2: usize = more.iter().map(|p| payload_len(p)).sum();
311
        assert_eq!(total2, 15);
312
        assert!(ch.out_is_empty());
313
    }
314
315
    #[test]
316
    fn incoming_window_replenishes_past_halfway() {
317
        let mut ch = Channel::new(0);
318
        // Below half → replenish back to full.
319
        assert_eq!(
320
            ch.consume_incoming(DEFAULT_WINDOW / 2 + 1),
321
            WindowUpdate::Ok(Some(DEFAULT_WINDOW / 2 + 1))
322
        );
323
        // Small consumption above half → no adjust.
324
        assert_eq!(ch.consume_incoming(1), WindowUpdate::Ok(None));
325
    }
326
327
    #[test]
328
    fn incoming_window_overflow_is_a_violation() {
329
        let mut ch = Channel::new(0);
330
        // The local window starts at DEFAULT_WINDOW; sending more is a violation.
331
        assert_eq!(
332
            ch.consume_incoming(DEFAULT_WINDOW + 1),
333
            WindowUpdate::Exceeded
334
        );
335
    }
336
337
    // length of the `data`/ext payload carried by a CHANNEL_DATA/EXTENDED_DATA message
338
    fn payload_len(p: &[u8]) -> usize {
339
        use crate::wire::Reader;
340
        let mut r = Reader::new(p);
341
        let id = r.u8().unwrap();
342
        r.u32().unwrap(); // recipient
343
        if id == msg::CHANNEL_EXTENDED_DATA {
344
            r.u32().unwrap(); // data type
345
        }
346
        r.string().unwrap().len()
347
    }
348
}
/home/nick/proj/rust/ssh-bench/crates/ssh-transport/src/error.rs
Line
Count
Source
1
//! Crate-wide error type. We deliberately avoid `anyhow`; every fallible path returns a
2
//! typed [`SshError`].
3
4
use core::fmt;
5
6
/// Result alias used throughout the engine.
7
pub type Result<T> = core::result::Result<T, SshError>;
8
9
/// All errors the protocol engine can produce.
10
#[derive(Debug)]
11
#[non_exhaustive]
12
pub enum SshError {
13
    /// The peer's identification string was missing, malformed, or an unsupported version.
14
    BadVersion(&'static str),
15
    /// A binary packet was malformed (bad length, padding, or truncated field).
16
    BadPacket(&'static str),
17
    /// Encoding or decoding of an SSH wire primitive failed.
18
    Encoding(&'static str),
19
    /// Algorithm negotiation found no algorithm in common for the named slot.
20
    NoCommonAlgorithm(&'static str),
21
    /// Key exchange failed (bad point, signature, or exchange-hash mismatch).
22
    Kex(&'static str),
23
    /// A key blob could not be parsed or encoded (e.g. an OpenSSH private key file).
24
    Key(&'static str),
25
    /// Payload (de)compression failed, or a decompressed payload exceeded the size bound.
26
    Compression(&'static str),
27
    /// Message integrity / AEAD authentication-tag verification failed.
28
    Integrity,
29
    /// Received a message that is not valid in the current protocol state.
30
    Protocol(&'static str),
31
    /// Authentication did not succeed.
32
    AuthFailed,
33
}
34
35
impl fmt::Display for SshError {
36
0
    fn fmt(&self, f: &mut fmt::Formatter<'_>) -> fmt::Result {
37
0
        match self {
38
0
            SshError::BadVersion(s) => write!(f, "bad SSH identification string: {s}"),
39
0
            SshError::BadPacket(s) => write!(f, "malformed binary packet: {s}"),
40
0
            SshError::Encoding(s) => write!(f, "wire encoding error: {s}"),
41
0
            SshError::NoCommonAlgorithm(s) => write!(f, "no common algorithm for {s}"),
42
0
            SshError::Kex(s) => write!(f, "key exchange failed: {s}"),
43
0
            SshError::Key(s) => write!(f, "key error: {s}"),
44
0
            SshError::Compression(s) => write!(f, "compression error: {s}"),
45
0
            SshError::Integrity => write!(f, "message integrity check failed"),
46
0
            SshError::Protocol(s) => write!(f, "protocol violation: {s}"),
47
0
            SshError::AuthFailed => write!(f, "authentication failed"),
48
        }
49
0
    }
50
}
51
52
impl core::error::Error for SshError {}

//! `ssh-ed25519` host keys: signing the exchange hash (server) and verifying it
2
//! (client), plus the SSH wire blob formats from RFC 8709.
3
//!
4
//! Public key blob `K_S`:  `string "ssh-ed25519"` ‖ `string key[32]`
5
//! Signature blob:         `string "ssh-ed25519"` ‖ `string sig[64]`
6
7
use ed25519_dalek::{Signer, SigningKey, VerifyingKey};
8
use rand_core::CryptoRngCore;
9
use ssh_key::private::{Ed25519Keypair, KeypairData};
10
use ssh_key::{LineEnding, PrivateKey};
11
use zeroize::Zeroizing;
12
13
use crate::algo::HOSTKEY_ED25519;
14
use crate::wire::{Reader, Writer};
15
use crate::{Result, SshError};
16
17
/// A private host key held by the server.
18
#[derive(Clone)]
19
pub struct HostKey {
20
    signing: SigningKey,
21
}
22
23

24
    /// Build from a 32-byte ed25519 seed (the private scalar seed).
25
0

26
0
        Self {
27
0
            signing: SigningKey::from_bytes(seed),
28
0
        }
29
0
    }
30
31
    /// Generate a fresh host key.
32
353

33
353

34
353
            signing: SigningKey::generate(rng),
35
353
        }
36
353
    }

38
    /// Parse an unencrypted OpenSSH-format private key (the
39
    /// `-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----` PEM written by `ssh-keygen -t ed25519`).
40
    /// Only `ssh-ed25519` keys are supported; encrypted keys are rejected.
41
0
    pub fn from_openssh(pem: &str) -> Result<Self> {
42
0
        let key = PrivateKey::from_openssh(pem)
43
0
            .map_err(|_| SshError::Key("invalid OpenSSH private key"))?;
44
0
        match key.key_data() {
45
0
            KeypairData::Ed25519(kp) => Ok(Self::from_seed(&kp.private.to_bytes())),
46
0
            _ => Err(SshError::Key(
47
0
                "host key is not ssh-ed25519 (or is encrypted)",
48
0
            )),
49
        }
50
0
    }
51
52
    /// Serialize to an unencrypted OpenSSH-format private key PEM, suitable for writing to
53
    /// a key file and reloading with [`HostKey::from_openssh`]. The PEM (and the transient
54
    /// seed used to build it) live in [`Zeroizing`] buffers so the private key text is
55
    /// scrubbed from memory once the returned value is dropped.
56
0
    pub fn to_openssh(&self) -> Result<Zeroizing<String>> {
57
0
        let seed = Zeroizing::new(self.signing.to_bytes());
58
0
        let kp = Ed25519Keypair::from_seed(&seed);
59
0
        PrivateKey::from(kp)
60
0
            .to_openssh(LineEnding::LF)
61
0
            .map_err(|_| SshError::Key("failed to encode host key"))
62
0
    }
63
64
    /// The public key blob `K_S` hashed into the exchange hash and sent to the client.
65
5
    pub fn public_blob(&self) -> Vec<u8> {
66
5
        public_blob(&self.signing.verifying_key())
67
5
    }
68
69
    /// The corresponding public key (for tests and known-host registration).
70
0
    pub fn public(&self) -> HostPublicKey {
71
0
        HostPublicKey {
72
0
            verifying: self.signing.verifying_key(),
73
0
        }
74
0
    }
75
76
    /// Sign the 32-byte exchange hash `H`, returning the SSH signature blob.
77
0
    pub fn sign_exchange_hash(&self, h: &[u8]) -> Vec<u8> {
78
0
        let sig = self.signing.sign(h);
79
0
        let mut w = Writer::new();
80
0
        w.string(HOSTKEY_ED25519.as_bytes());
81
0
        w.string(&sig.to_bytes());
82
0
        w.into_bytes()
83
0
    }
84
}
85
86
/// A public host key held/seen by the client.
87
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
88
pub struct HostPublicKey {
89
    verifying: VerifyingKey,
90
}
91
92
impl HostPublicKey {
93
    /// Parse a `K_S` public key blob.
94
0
    pub fn parse_blob(blob: &[u8]) -> Result<Self> {
95
0
        let mut r = Reader::new(blob);
96
0
        if r.utf8()? != HOSTKEY_ED25519 {
97
0
            return Err(SshError::Kex("unsupported host key algorithm"));
98
0
        }
99
0
        let key = r.string()?;
100
0
        let key: [u8; 32] = key
101
0
            .try_into()
102
0
            .map_err(|_| SshError::Kex("ed25519 key is not 32 bytes"))?;
103
0
        let verifying =
104
0
            VerifyingKey::from_bytes(&key).map_err(|_| SshError::Kex("invalid ed25519 key"))?;
105
0
        Ok(Self { verifying })
106
0
    }
107
108
    /// Build from a raw 32-byte ed25519 public key.
109
0
    pub fn from_ed25519_bytes(bytes: &[u8; 32]) -> Result<Self> {
110
        Ok(Self {
111
0
            verifying: VerifyingKey::from_bytes(bytes)
112
0
                .map_err(|_| SshError::Kex("invalid ed25519 key"))?,
113
        })
114
0
    }
115
116
    /// The `K_S` public key blob for this key.
117
0
    pub fn blob(&self) -> Vec<u8> {
118
0
        public_blob(&self.verifying)
119
0
    }
120
121
    /// Verify a signature blob over the exchange hash `H`.
122
0
    pub fn verify(&self, h: &[u8], sig_blob: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
123
0
        let mut r = Reader::new(sig_blob);
124
0

125
0
            return Err(SshError::Kex("unsupported signature algorithm"));
126
0

127
0
        let sig = r.string()?;
128
0
        let sig: [u8; 64] = sig
129
0
            .try_into()
130
0
            .map_err(|_| SshError::Kex("ed25519 signature is not 64 bytes"))?;
131
0
        let signature = ed25519_dalek::Signature::from_bytes(&sig);
132
0
        self.verifying
133
0
            .verify_strict(h, &signature)
134
0
            .map_err(|_| SshError::Kex("host key signature verification failed"))
135
0
    }
136
}
137
138
5
fn public_blob(vk: &VerifyingKey) -> Vec<u8> {
139
5
    let mut w = Writer::new();
140
5
    w.string(HOSTKEY_ED25519.as_bytes());
141
5
    w.string(vk.as_bytes());
142
5
    w.into_bytes()
143
5
}

145
#[cfg(test)]
146
mod tests {
147
    use super::*;
148
    use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng;
149
    use rand_core::SeedableRng;
150
151
    #[test]
152
    fn sign_then_verify_roundtrip() {
153
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(42);
154
        let host = HostKey::generate(&mut rng);
155
        let h = [0x11u8; 32];
156
        let sig = host.sign_exchange_hash(&h);
157
158
        let pubkey = HostPublicKey::parse_blob(&host.public_blob()).unwrap();
159
        assert!(pubkey.verify(&h, &sig).is_ok());
160
    }
161
162
    #[test]
163
    fn verify_rejects_tampered_hash() {
164
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(43);
165
        let host = HostKey::generate(&mut rng);
166
        let sig = host.sign_exchange_hash(&[0x11u8; 32]);
167
        let pubkey = host.public();
168
        assert!(matches!(
169
            pubkey.verify(&[0x22u8; 32], &sig),
170
            Err(SshError::Kex(_))
171
        ));
172
    }
173
174
    #[test]
175
    fn openssh_pem_roundtrip_preserves_key() {
176
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(7);
177
        let host = HostKey::generate(&mut rng);
178
        let pem = host.to_openssh().unwrap();
179
        assert!(pem.starts_with("-----BEGIN OPENSSH PRIVATE KEY-----"));
180
181
        // Reloading yields the same public identity and a usable signing key.
182
        let reloaded = HostKey::from_openssh(&pem).unwrap();
183
        assert_eq!(reloaded.public(), host.public());
184
        let h = [0x33u8; 32];
185
        let sig = reloaded.sign_exchange_hash(&h);
186
        assert!(host.public().verify(&h, &sig).is_ok());
187
    }
188
189
    #[test]
190
    fn from_openssh_rejects_garbage() {
191
        assert!(matches!(
192
            HostKey::from_openssh("not a key"),
193
            Err(SshError::Key(_))
194
        ));
195
    }
196
197
    #[test]
198
    fn parse_blob_rejects_wrong_algorithm() {
199
        let mut w = Writer::new();
200
        w.string(b"ssh-rsa");
201
        w.string(&[0u8; 32]);
202
        assert!(matches!(
203
            HostPublicKey::parse_blob(&w.into_bytes()),
204
            Err(SshError::Kex(_))
205
        ));
206
    }
207
}
/home/nick/proj/rust/ssh-bench/crates/ssh-transport/src/kdf.rs
Line
Count
Source
1
//! Exchange-hash computation and key derivation for `curve25519-sha256`
2
//! (RFC 4253 §7.2, RFC 8731). The hash is SHA-256.
3
4
use sha2::{Digest, Sha256};
5
use zeroize::{Zeroize, ZeroizeOnDrop};
6
7
use crate::wire::Writer;
8
9
/// Inputs to the exchange hash `H`.
10
pub struct ExchangeHashInput<'a> {
11
    /// `V_C` — client identification string (no CR LF).
12
    pub client_id: &'a [u8],
13
    /// `V_S` — server identification string (no CR LF).
14
    pub server_id: &'a [u8],
15
    /// `I_C` — client's KEXINIT payload.
16
    pub client_kexinit: &'a [u8],
17
    /// `I_S` — server's KEXINIT payload.
18
    pub server_kexinit: &'a [u8],
19
    /// `K_S` — server host key blob.
20
    pub host_key_blob: &'a [u8],
21
    /// `Q_C` — client ephemeral public value.
22
    pub client_ephemeral: &'a [u8],
23
    /// `Q_S` — server ephemeral public value.
24
    pub server_ephemeral: &'a [u8],
25
    /// `K` — shared secret, as unsigned big-endian magnitude.
26
    pub shared_secret: &'a [u8],
27
}
28
29
/// Compute the exchange hash `H = SHA256(V_C ‖ V_S ‖ I_C ‖ I_S ‖ K_S ‖ Q_C ‖ Q_S ‖ K)`
30
/// where every component is SSH-encoded (`string` for all but `K`, which is `mpint`).
31
0
pub fn exchange_hash(input: &ExchangeHashInput<'_>) -> [u8; 32] {
32
0
    let mut w = Writer::new();
33
0
    w.string(input.client_id);
34
0
    w.string(input.server_id);
35
0
    w.string(input.client_kexinit);
36
0
    w.string(input.server_kexinit);
37
0
    w.string(input.host_key_blob);
38
0
    w.string(input.client_ephemeral);
39
0
    w.string(input.server_ephemeral);
40
0
    w.mpint(input.shared_secret);
41
0
    let h = Sha256::digest(w.as_slice()).into();
42
    // The hash input ends with the shared secret K; scrub the buffer before it is freed.
43
0
    w.into_bytes().zeroize();
44
0
    h
45
0
}
46
47
/// The directional keys/IVs derived from a completed key exchange. `chacha20-poly1305`
48
/// uses only the encryption keys (IVs derived with length 0); `aes256-gcm` uses both.
49
/// The key bytes are scrubbed once the `Keys` are dropped (after the ciphers copy them).
50
#[derive(Zeroize, ZeroizeOnDrop)]
51
pub struct Keys {
52
    /// Initial IV, client-to-server (letter `A`).
53
    pub iv_c2s: Vec<u8>,
54
    /// Initial IV, server-to-client (letter `B`).
55
    pub iv_s2c: Vec<u8>,
56
    /// Encryption key, client-to-server (letter `C`).
57
    pub enc_c2s: Vec<u8>,
58
    /// Encryption key, server-to-client (letter `D`).
59
    pub enc_s2c: Vec<u8>,
60
}
61
62
impl Keys {
63
    /// Derive `key_len` encryption-key bytes and `iv_len` IV bytes per direction.
64
0
    pub fn derive(
65
0
        shared_secret: &[u8],
66
0
        h: &[u8; 32],
67
0
