68
0
        key_len: usize,
69
0
        iv_len: usize,
70
0
    ) -> Self {
71
0
        let mut k_mpint = mpint_bytes(shared_secret);
72
0

73
0

74
0
            iv_s2c: derive_key(&k_mpint, h, b'B', session_id, iv_len),
75
0
            enc_c2s: derive_key(&k_mpint, h, b'C', session_id, key_len),
76
0
            enc_s2c: derive_key(&k_mpint, h, b'D', session_id, key_len),
77
0
        };
78
        // `k_mpint` holds the shared secret K; scrub it now that derivation is done.
79
0
        k_mpint.zeroize();
80
0
        keys
81
0
    }
82
}
83
84
/// `K1 = HASH(K ‖ H ‖ X ‖ session_id)`, then `K_{n+1} = HASH(K ‖ H ‖ K1 ‖ .. ‖ Kn)`,
85
/// concatenated and truncated to `out_len`.
86
0
fn derive_key(
87
0
    k_mpint: &[u8],
88
0
    h: &[u8; 32],
89
0
    letter: u8,
90
0
    session_id: &[u8],
91
0
    out_len: usize,
92
0
) -> Vec<u8> {
93
0
    let mut out: Vec<u8> = Vec::with_capacity(out_len);
94
95
0

96
0
    first.update(k_mpint);
97
0
    first.update(h);
98
0
    first.update([letter]);
99
0

100
0

101
102
0
    while out.len() < out_len {
103
0

104
0
        next.update(k_mpint);
105
0
        next.update(h);
106
0
        next.update(&out);
107
0
        out.extend_from_slice(&next.finalize());
108
0
    }
109
0
    out.truncate(out_len);
110
0
    out
111
0
}
112
113
/// Encode an unsigned big-endian magnitude as an SSH `mpint` (sign byte + trimmed).
114
0
fn mpint_bytes(magnitude: &[u8]) -> Vec<u8> {
115
0
    let mut w = Writer::new();
116
0
    w.mpint(magnitude);
117
0
    w.into_bytes()
118
0
}
119
120
#[cfg(test)]
121
mod tests {
122
    use super::*;
123
124
    #[test]
125
    fn derive_produces_requested_length_with_extension() {
126
        // 64 bytes needs the chained extension (two SHA-256 blocks).
127
        let keys = Keys::derive(&[0x42u8; 32], &[0x11u8; 32], &[0x33u8; 32], 64, 12);
128
        assert_eq!(keys.enc_c2s.len(), 64);
129
        assert_eq!(keys.enc_s2c.len(), 64);
130
        assert_eq!(keys.iv_c2s.len(), 12);
131
        // Different letters must give different material.
132
        assert_ne!(keys.enc_c2s, keys.enc_s2c);
133
        assert_ne!(keys.iv_c2s, keys.iv_s2c);
134
    }
135
136
    #[test]
137
    fn derive_is_deterministic() {
138
        let a = Keys::derive(&[1u8; 32], &[2u8; 32], &[3u8; 32], 64, 0);
139
        let b = Keys::derive(&[1u8; 32], &[2u8; 32], &[3u8; 32], 64, 0);
140
        assert_eq!(a.enc_c2s, b.enc_c2s);
141
    }
142
143
    #[test]
144
    fn exchange_hash_changes_with_inputs() {
145
        let base = ExchangeHashInput {
146
            client_id: b"SSH-2.0-a",
147
            server_id: b"SSH-2.0-b",
148
            client_kexinit: b"ic",
149
            server_kexinit: b"is",
150
            host_key_blob: b"ks",
151
            client_ephemeral: &[1u8; 32],
152
            server_ephemeral: &[2u8; 32],
153
            shared_secret: &[3u8; 32],
154
        };
155
        let h1 = exchange_hash(&base);
156
        let other = ExchangeHashInput {
157
            shared_secret: &[4u8; 32],
158
            ..base
159
        };
160
        let h2 = exchange_hash(&other);
161
        assert_ne!(h1, h2);
162
    }
163
}

9
/// An ephemeral X25519 key pair. The public value is the 32-byte `Q` sent on the wire.

#[cfg(test)]
43
mod tests {
44
    use super::*;
45
    use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng;
46
    use rand_core::SeedableRng;
47
48
    #[test]
49
    fn both_sides_agree_on_shared_secret() {
50
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(1);
51
        let client = EcdhKeypair::generate(&mut rng);
52
        let server = EcdhKeypair::generate(&mut rng);
53
        let cpub = client.public();
54
        let spub = server.public();
55
        let k_client = client.agree(&spub).unwrap();
56
        let k_server = server.agree(&cpub).unwrap();
57
        assert_eq!(*k_client, *k_server);
58
    }
59
60
    #[test]
61
    fn rejects_wrong_length_peer_key() {
62
        let mut rng = ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(2);
63
        let kp = EcdhKeypair::generate(&mut rng);
64
        assert!(matches!(kp.agree(&[0u8; 31]), Err(SshError::Kex(_))));
65
    }
66
}

//! Binary Packet Protocol framing, RFC 4253 §6.
2
//!
3
//! ```text
4
//! uint32    packet_length   (length of the rest, excluding the MAC)
5
//! byte      padding_length
6
//! byte[n1]  payload         (n1 = packet_length - padding_length - 1)
7
//! byte[n2]  random padding  (n2 = padding_length)
8
//! byte[m]   mac             (only once a MAC/AEAD is in effect)
9
//! ```
10
//!
11
//! This module handles the unencrypted framing used before `NEWKEYS`. The encrypted
12
//! AEAD framing (chacha20-poly1305) is layered on top in the cipher module during M1.
13
14
use rand_core::RngCore;
15
16
use crate::{Result, SshError};
17
18
/// Minimum padding length (RFC 4253 §6).
19
pub const MIN_PADDING: usize = 4;
20
/// Minimum total packet size including the length field (RFC 4253 §6).
21
pub const MIN_PACKET: usize = 16;
22
/// Block size used to align unencrypted packets ("none" cipher): 8.
23
const BLOCK: usize = 8;
24
/// Defensive upper bound on `packet_length` to bound memory use.
25
pub const MAX_PACKET_LENGTH: usize = 1024 * 1024;
26
27
/// Compute the padding length for a payload so the whole packet is a multiple of
28
/// `block` (>= 8), padding is >= 4, and the total is >= 16.
29
353
pub(crate) fn padding_len(payload_len: usize, block: usize) -> usize {
30
353
    let block = block.max(BLOCK);
31
353
    let unpadded = 4 + 1 + payload_len;
32
353
    let mut pad = block - (unpadded % block);
33
353
    if pad < MIN_PADDING {
34
353
        pad += block;
35
353
    }
36
353
    while 4 + 1 + payload_len + pad < MIN_PACKET {
37
0
        pad += block;
38
0
    }
39
353
    pad
40
353
}
41
42
/// Encode `payload` into an unencrypted binary packet, drawing random padding from `rng`.
43
353
pub fn encode_plain(payload: &[u8], rng: &mut impl RngCore) -> Vec<u8> {
44
353
    let pad = padding_len(payload.len(), BLOCK);
45
353
    let packet_length = 1 + payload.len() + pad;
46
353
    let mut out = Vec::with_capacity(4 + packet_length);
47
353
    out.extend_from_slice(&(packet_length as u32).to_be_bytes());
48
353
    out.push(pad as u8);
49
353
    out.extend_from_slice(payload);
50
353
    let pad_start = out.len();
51
353
    out.resize(pad_start + pad, 0);
52
353
    rng.fill_bytes(&mut out[pad_start..]);
53
353
    out
54
353
}

/// Try to decode one unencrypted packet from the front of `buf`.
57
///
58

59
/// `consumed` bytes), `Ok(None)` when more bytes are required, or an error for a
60
/// malformed packet.
61
931
pub fn decode_plain(buf: &[u8]) -> Result<Option<(Vec<u8>, usize)>> {
62
931

63
40

64
891

891

66
891

868

868

71
13

72
855

855

74
855

852

852

78
852

79
852

#[cfg(test)]
83
mod tests {
84
    use super::*;
85
    use rand_chacha::ChaCha8Rng;
86
    use rand_core::SeedableRng;
87
88
    fn rng() -> ChaCha8Rng {
89
        ChaCha8Rng::seed_from_u64(0xA5A5)
90
    }
91
92
    #[test]
93
    fn padding_keeps_packet_block_aligned_and_minimum() {
94
        for payload_len in 0..64usize {
95
            let pad = padding_len(payload_len, BLOCK);
96
            let total = 4 + 1 + payload_len + pad;
97
            assert_eq!(total % BLOCK, 0, "not block aligned for {payload_len}");
98
            assert!(pad >= MIN_PADDING);
99
            assert!(total >= MIN_PACKET);
100
        }
101
    }
102
103
    #[test]
104
    fn roundtrip_small_and_large() {
105
        let mut rng = rng();
106
        for payload in [
107
            vec![],
108
            vec![20u8],
109
            b"the quick brown fox".to_vec(),
110
            vec![7u8; 5000],
111
        ] {
112
            let frame = encode_plain(&payload, &mut rng);
113
            let (decoded, consumed) = decode_plain(&frame).unwrap().unwrap();
114
            assert_eq!(decoded, payload);
115
            assert_eq!(consumed, frame.len());
116
        }
117
    }
118
119
    #[test]
120
    fn decode_needs_more_bytes() {
121
        let mut rng = rng();
122
        let frame = encode_plain(b"hello", &mut rng);
123
        assert_eq!(decode_plain(&frame[..3]).unwrap(), None);
124
        assert_eq!(decode_plain(&frame[..frame.len() - 1]).unwrap(), None);
125
    }
126
127
    #[test]
128
    fn decode_reports_trailing_bytes_via_consumed() {
129
        let mut rng = rng();
130
        let mut frame = encode_plain(b"hello", &mut rng);
131
        let original_len = frame.len();
132
        frame.extend_from_slice(b"next-packet-bytes");
133
        let (decoded, consumed) = decode_plain(&frame).unwrap().unwrap();
134
        assert_eq!(decoded, b"hello");
135
        assert_eq!(consumed, original_len);
136
    }
137
138
    #[test]
139
    fn rejects_bad_padding_length() {
140
        // packet_length = 12, padding_length = 1 (< MIN_PADDING)
141
        let mut buf = vec![0, 0, 0, 12, 1];
142
        buf.extend_from_slice(&[0u8; 11]);
143
        assert!(matches!(decode_plain(&buf), Err(SshError::BadPacket(_))));
144
    }
145
146
    #[test]
147
    fn rejects_oversize_packet_length() {
148
        let buf = (MAX_PACKET_LENGTH as u32 + 1).to_be_bytes().to_vec();
149
        assert!(matches!(decode_plain(&buf), Err(SshError::BadPacket(_))));
150
    }
151
}

//! Server-side session: wraps the [`Transport`] and drives user authentication
2
//! (RFC 4252) and the connection protocol (RFC 4254). Authentication *policy* is
3
//! delegated to a [`ServerAuthHandler`]; process spawning for `exec`/`shell` is the
4
//! caller's job, driven by the [`ServerEvent`]s emitted here.

/// High-level server events.

#[derive(Debug)]
41
pub enum ServerEvent {
42
    /// Authentication succeeded for `user`.
43
    Authenticated { user: Box<str> },
44
    /// The client requested command execution on `channel`.
45
    ExecRequest { channel: u32, command: Box<str> },
46
    /// The client requested an interactive shell on `channel`.
47
    ShellRequest { channel: u32 },
48
    /// The client requested a subsystem (e.g. `sftp`) on `channel`.
49
    SubsystemRequest { channel: u32, name: Box<str> },
50
    /// Channel data from the client (process stdin).
51
    ChannelData { channel: u32, data: Vec<u8> },
52
    /// The client sent EOF (no more stdin).
53
    ChannelEof { channel: u32 },
54
    /// The channel was closed.
55
    ChannelClose { channel: u32 },
56
    /// The peer disconnected.
57
    Disconnect { reason: u32, description: Box<str> },
58
    /// Authentication failed too many times; the server has sent `SSH_MSG_DISCONNECT`
59
    /// and the connection is finished. A driver can use this to record/ban the peer.
60
    AuthExhausted,
61
}
62
63
/// A server-side SSH connection (single session channel).
64
pub struct ServerConnection<R: RngCore + CryptoRng, H: ServerAuthHandler> {
65
    transport: Transport<R>,
66
    handler: H,
67
    authenticated: Option<Box<str>>,
68
    banner_sent: bool,
69
    channel: Option<Channel>,
70
    /// `want_reply` of a deferred exec/shell/subsystem request awaiting accept/reject.
71
    pending_reply: bool,
72
    /// Count of failed authentication attempts (the brute-force cap).
73
    auth_failures: u32,
74
    events: std::collections::VecDeque<ServerEvent>,
75
}
76
77
/// Local channel id we assign (a single session channel is supported).
78
const LOCAL_CHANNEL: u32 = 0;
79
80
impl<R: RngCore + CryptoRng, H: ServerAuthHandler> ServerConnection<R, H> {

135
0

0

137
0

138
0

139
0

149
    /// Mark the process as exited with `status`; flushes remaining output, then sends
150

151
0

0

0

154
0

155
0
        }
156
0

157
0
    }

159
0
    fn channel_for(&mut self, channel: u32) -> Option<&mut Channel> {
160
0
        self.channel.as_mut().filter(|ch| ch.local_id == channel)
161
0
    }

    /// Accept a pending exec/shell/subsystem request, sending `CHANNEL_SUCCESS` if the
397
    /// client asked for a reply. Call before producing any output for the request.
398
0
    pub fn accept_channel(&mut self, _channel: u32) -> Result<()> {

0

400
0

401
0
            self.transport.send_packet(&conn::channel_success(remote))?;
402
0
        }
403
0

404
0
    }
405
406

0

/home/nick/proj/rust/ssh-bench/crates/ssh-transport/src/transport.rs
Line
Count
Source
1
//! The sans-IO SSH transport state machine (RFC 4253): identification exchange,
2
//! `KEXINIT` negotiation, `curve25519-sha256` key exchange, `NEWKEYS`, and the switch
3
//! to authenticated encryption. Drives both the client and server roles.
4
//!
5
//! The driver feeds socket bytes via [`Transport::on_input`], drains bytes to write via
6
//! [`Transport::take_output`], and pulls high-level [`Event`]s via
7
//! [`Transport::poll_event`]. After the handshake, application layers exchange packets
8
//! through [`Transport::send_packet`] and [`Event::Packet`].
9
10
use std::collections::VecDeque;
11
12
use rand_core::{CryptoRng, RngCore};
13
use zeroize::Zeroizing;
14
15
use crate::algo::{self, COMPRESSION_ZLIB_OPENSSH, KexInit, Negotiated};
16
use crate::cipher::Cipher;
17
use crate::compress::{Compressor, Decompressor};
18
use crate::hostkey::{HostKey, HostPublicKey};
19
use crate::kdf::{self, ExchangeHashInput};
20
use crate::kex::EcdhKeypair;
21
use crate::version;
22
use crate::wire::{Reader, Writer};
23
use crate::{Result, SshError, msg};
24
25
/// Connection role.
26
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
27
pub enum Role {
28
    Client,
29
    Server,
30
}
31
32
/// High-level events surfaced to the driver.
33
#[derive(Debug)]
34
pub enum Event {
35
    /// Client only: the server's host key. The driver must enforce known-hosts policy
36
    /// and call [`Transport::disconnect`] if it is not trusted. The signature over the
37
    /// exchange hash has already been cryptographically verified.
38
    ServerHostKey(HostPublicKey),
39
    /// The secure transport is established; `session_id` is now available.
40
    Established,
41
    /// A decrypted application-layer packet (auth/connection protocol payload). Held in
42
    /// a [`Zeroizing`] buffer so the plaintext (which may carry a password) is scrubbed
43
    /// from memory once the consuming layer drops it.
44
    Packet(Zeroizing<Vec<u8>>),
45
    /// The peer sent `SSH_MSG_DISCONNECT`.
46
    Disconnect { reason: u32, description: Box<str> },
47
}
48
49
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
50
enum Phase {
51
    NeedPeerVersion,
52
    Handshake,
53
    Established,
54
}
55
56
/// Application bytes after which we proactively re-key (RFC 4253 §9 suggests ~1 GiB).
57
const REKEY_BYTES: u64 = 1 << 30;
58
59
/// Default for [`Transport::set_max_consecutive_peer_rekeys`]: how many peer-initiated
60
/// re-keys we tolerate with no application traffic in between before treating it as a
61
/// re-key flood (key exchange is CPU-heavy, so a peer spamming `KEXINIT` is a cheap
62
/// asymmetric DoS). The counter resets whenever an application packet arrives, so normal
63
/// use — which always interleaves data — is never affected.
64
pub const DEFAULT_MAX_CONSECUTIVE_PEER_REKEYS: u32 = 3;
65
66
/// Pending new directional ciphers, installed at the corresponding `NEWKEYS`.
67
struct PendingKeys {
68
    out: Cipher,
69
    inn: Cipher,
70
}
71
72
/// The SSH transport engine.
73
pub struct Transport<R: RngCore + CryptoRng> {
74
    role: Role,
75
    rng: R,
76
    phase: Phase,
77
78
    rx: Vec<u8>,
79
    tx: Vec<u8>,
80
    tx_seq: u32,
81
    rx_seq: u32,
82
    cipher_out: Cipher,
83
    cipher_in: Cipher,
84
    comp_out: Compressor,
85
    comp_in: Decompressor,
86
    /// Negotiated compression names for the current key epoch (directional).
87
    comp_out_name: Box<str>,
88
    comp_in_name: Box<str>,
89
    /// Whether delayed compression (`zlib@openssh.com`) has engaged (post-auth).
90
    comp_active: bool,
91
92
    local_id: Vec<u8>,
93
    peer_id: Option<Vec<u8>>,
94
    local_kexinit: Vec<u8>,
95
    peer_kexinit: Option<KexInit>,
96
    negotiated: Option<Negotiated>,
97
    ecdh: Option<EcdhKeypair>,
98
    pending: Option<PendingKeys>,
99
    sent_newkeys: bool,
100
    recv_newkeys: bool,
101
    skip_guess: bool,
102
    /// Strict KEX (Terrapin mitigation): reset sequence numbers at NEWKEYS and forbid
103
    /// non-KEX messages during the initial exchange. Enabled when both peers advertise.
104
    strict_kex: bool,
105
    /// Set once the first key exchange completes (the connection is up). Sequence-number
106
    /// resets under strict KEX apply only to that initial exchange, not to rekeys.
107
    initial_kex_done: bool,
108
    /// A re-key is in progress: we have sent our KEXINIT but not yet our NEWKEYS, so
109
    /// application packets are queued rather than sent.
110
    rekeying: bool,
111
    /// Whether our KEXINIT for the current round has been sent.
112
    kexinit_sent: bool,
113
    /// Application packets deferred while [`Self::rekeying`].
114
    tx_app_queue: VecDeque<Vec<u8>>,
115
    /// Application-payload bytes sent since the last key exchange (auto-rekey trigger).
116
    bytes_since_rekey: u64,
117
    /// Peer-initiated re-keys since the last application packet (re-key flood guard).
118
    consecutive_peer_rekeys: u32,
119
    /// Tolerated burst before a re-key flood is treated as abuse (settable by user code).
120
    max_consecutive_peer_rekeys: u32,
121
    session_id: Option<[u8; 32]>,
122
123
    host_key: Option<HostKey>,
124
    events: VecDeque<Event>,
125
    /// Cipher names to offer (preference order), or `None` for the default set. Preserved
126
    /// across rekeys so a pinned preference stays in effect.
127
    offered_ciphers: Option<Vec<Box<str>>>,
128
    /// Compression names to offer (preference order), or `None` for the default set.
129
    offered_compression: Option<Vec<Box<str>>>,
130
    /// Set once we have queued our own `SSH_MSG_DISCONNECT`. No further peer input is
131
    /// processed; the driver should flush the queued bytes and close the connection.
132
    closing: bool,
133
}
134
135
impl<R: RngCore + CryptoRng> Transport<R> {
136
    /// Start a client transport, queuing our identification and KEXINIT.
137
0
    pub fn new_client(rng: R) -> Self {
138
0
        Self::start(Role::Client, rng, None, None, None)
139
0
    }
140
141
    /// Start a client transport offering `ciphers` (preference order) instead of the
142
    /// default set. Negotiation prefers the client's order, so this pins which cipher is
143
    /// selected when the server supports it.
144
0
    pub fn new_client_with_ciphers(rng: R, ciphers: &[&str]) -> Self {
145
0
        let pref = ciphers.iter().map(|s| Box::from(*s)).collect();
146
0
        Self::start(Role::Client, rng, None, Some(pref), None)
147
0
    }
148
149
    /// Start a client transport offering `compression` (preference order). Negotiation
150
    /// prefers the client's order, so listing `zlib@openssh.com` first turns on delayed
151
    /// compression when the server supports it.
152
0
    pub fn new_client_with_compression(rng: R, compression: &[&str]) -> Self {
153
0
        let pref = compression.iter().map(|s| Box::from(*s)).collect();
154
0
        Self::start(Role::Client, rng, None, None, Some(pref))
155
0
    }
156
157
    /// Start a server transport with the given host key.
158
353
    pub fn new_server(rng: R, host_key: HostKey) -> Self {
159
353
        Self::start(Role::Server, rng, Some(host_key), None, None)
160
353
    }

158
353
    pub fn new_server(rng: R, host_key: HostKey) -> Self {
159
353
        Self::start(Role::Server, rng, Some(host_key), None, None)
160
353
    }

213
214
    /// Build and queue our KEXINIT for the current key-exchange round.
215
353
    fn send_kexinit(&mut self) {
216
353
        let is_server = self.role == Role::Server;
217
353
        let ciphers: Vec<&str> = match &self.offered_ciphers {
218
0
            Some(pref) => pref.iter().map(|s| &**s).collect(),
219
353
            None => algo::default_ciphers().to_vec(),
220
        };
221
353
        let compressions: Vec<&str> = match &self.offered_compression {
222
0
            Some(pref) => pref.iter().map(|s| &**s).collect(),
223
353
            None => algo::default_compressions().to_vec(),
224
        };
225
353
        let ki = KexInit::ours_with(&mut self.rng, is_server, &ciphers, &compressions);
226
353
        self.local_kexinit = ki.payload;
227
353
        let payload = self.local_kexinit.clone();
228
353
        self.write_packet(&payload);
229
353
        self.kexinit_sent = true;
230

    /// Derive directional keys and stage the post-NEWKEYS ciphers.
663
0
    fn finish_kex(&mut self, h: [u8; 32], shared: &[u8]) -> Result<()> {
664
        // First key exchange fixes the session id.
665
0
        let session_id = *self.session_id.get_or_insert(h);
666
0
        let cipher = self.negotiated.as_ref().unwrap().cipher_c2s.clone();
667
0

668
0

669
0
        let keys = kdf::Keys::derive(shared, &h, &session_id, key_len, iv_len);
670
671
0
        let (out_key, out_iv, in_key, in_iv) = match self.role {
672
0
            Role::Client => (&keys.enc_c2s, &keys.iv_c2s, &keys.enc_s2c, &keys.iv_s2c),
673
0
            Role::Server => (&keys.enc_s2c, &keys.iv_s2c, &keys.enc_c2s, &keys.iv_c2s),
674
        };
675
0
        self.pending = Some(PendingKeys {
676
0
            out: Cipher::new(&cipher, out_key, out_iv)?,
677
0
            inn: Cipher::new(&cipher, in_key, in_iv)?,
678
        });
679
680
        // Record the negotiated compression names for this epoch (directional). The
681
        // contexts themselves are (re)installed at NEWKEYS, in step with the cipher.
682
0

683
0

684
0
            Role::Client => (n.comp_c2s.clone(), n.comp_s2c.clone()),
685
0
            Role::Server => (n.comp_s2c.clone(), n.comp_c2s.clone()),
686
        };
687
0
        self.comp_out_name = out_name;
688
0
        self.comp_in_name = in_name;
689
0
        Ok(())
690
0
    }

    fn send_newkeys(&mut self) {
693
0
        self.write_packet(&[msg::NEWKEYS]);
694
        // The next outbound packet uses the new cipher. Under strict KEX the send
695
        // sequence number resets to zero after *every* NEWKEYS (initial and rekey) —
696
        // OpenSSH does the same, so we must match to stay in sync.
697
0
        if let Some(p) = self.pending.as_mut() {
698
0
            self.cipher_out = core::mem::replace(&mut p.out, Cipher::None);
699
0
        }
700
        // Compression context resets per key exchange (RFC 4253 §6.2). Only re-install it
701
        // if delayed compression has already engaged; otherwise it stays off until auth.
702
0
        if self.comp_active {
703
0
            self.comp_out = Compressor::new(&self.comp_out_name);
704
0
        }
705
0
        if self.strict_kex {
706
0
            self.tx_seq = 0;
707
0
        }
708
0
        self.sent_newkeys = true;
709

    /// Once both NEWKEYS are exchanged, finish the round: establish the connection (on

738

/home/nick/proj/rust/ssh-bench/crates/ssh-transport/src/version.rs

Source
1
//! SSH protocol version (identification string) exchange, RFC 4253 §4.2.
2
//!
3
//! Each side sends a single line `SSH-2.0-<softwareversion>[ <comments>]` terminated
4
//! by CR LF. The server may precede it with arbitrary banner lines; the client must
5
//! tolerate and skip them. The identification line (without CR LF) is fed verbatim
6
//! into the key-exchange hash, so we preserve the exact bytes.
7
8
use crate::{Result, SshError};
9
10
/// Our software identification, without the trailing CR LF.
11
pub const LOCAL_ID: &str = "SSH-2.0-rust_ssh_0.1.0";
12
13
/// Maximum length of a single line including CR LF (RFC 4253 §4.2).
14
const MAX_LINE: usize = 255;
15
16
/// The peer's parsed identification line (the `SSH-2.0-...` bytes, no CR LF).
17
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)]
18
pub struct PeerId {
19
    pub raw: Vec<u8>,
20
}
21
22
/// Bytes to send for our identification line, including the terminating CR LF.
23
353
pub fn local_id_line() -> Vec<u8> {
24
353
    let mut v = Vec::with_capacity(LOCAL_ID.len() + 2);
25
353
    v.extend_from_slice(LOCAL_ID.as_bytes());
26
353
    v.extend_from_slice(b"\r\n");
27
353
    v
28
353
}
29
30
/// Attempt to parse the peer identification from a receive buffer.
31
///
32
/// Returns `Ok(Some((peer, consumed)))` once a full `SSH-2.0`/`SSH-1.99` line has
33
/// arrived (`consumed` bytes should be drained from the buffer), `Ok(None)` if more
34
/// bytes are needed, or an error for a malformed or over-long line.
35
///
36
/// `allow_banner` should be `true` for the client (servers may emit banner lines
37
/// before their identification) and `false` for the server.
38
353
pub fn parse_peer_id(buf: &[u8], allow_banner: bool) -> Result<Option<(PeerId, usize)>> {
39
353
    let mut start = 0;
40
    loop {
41
4.57k
        let Some(rel_nl) = buf[start..].iter().position(|&b| b == b'\n') else {
42
            // No complete line yet; guard against an unbounded pre-identification flood.
43
11
            if buf.len() - start > MAX_LINE * 64 {
44
0
                return Err(SshError::BadVersion("banner too long"));
45
11
            }
46
11
            return Ok(None);
47
        };
48
342
        let nl = start + rel_nl;
49
342
        if nl - start + 1 > MAX_LINE {
50
3
            return Err(SshError::BadVersion("identification line too long"));
51
339
        }
52
        // Line content without the LF, and without a preceding CR if present.
53
339
        let mut line_end = nl;
54
339
        if line_end > start && buf[line_end - 1] == b'\r' {
55
0
            line_end -= 1;
56
339
        }
57
339
        let line = &buf[start..line_end];
58
59
339
        if line.starts_with(b"SSH-2.0-") || line.starts_with(b"SSH-1.99-") {
60
332
            return Ok(Some((PeerId { raw: line.to_vec() }, nl + 1)));
61
7
        }
62
63
7
        if !allow_banner {
64
7
            return Err(SshError::BadVersion("expected SSH identification string"));
65
0
        }
66
        // Skip this banner line and look for the next one.
67
0
        start = nl + 1;
68
    }
69
353
}
70
71
#[cfg(test)]
72
mod tests {
73
    use super::*;
74
75
    #[test]
76
    fn parses_plain_identification() {
77
        let buf = b"SSH-2.0-OpenSSH_9.6\r\n";
78
        let (peer, consumed) = parse_peer_id(buf, true).unwrap().unwrap();
79
        assert_eq!(peer.raw, b"SSH-2.0-OpenSSH_9.6");
80
        assert_eq!(consumed, buf.len());
81
    }
82
83
    #[test]
84
    fn tolerates_lf_without_cr() {
85
        let buf = b"SSH-2.0-foo\n";
86
        let (peer, consumed) = parse_peer_id(buf, true).unwrap().unwrap();
87
        assert_eq!(peer.raw, b"SSH-2.0-foo");
88
        assert_eq!(consumed, buf.len());
89
    }
90
91
    #[test]
92
    fn client_skips_banner_lines() {
93
        let buf = b"hello there\r\nlegal notice\r\nSSH-2.0-OpenSSH_9.6\r\n";
94
        let (peer, consumed) = parse_peer_id(buf, true).unwrap().unwrap();
95
        assert_eq!(peer.raw, b"SSH-2.0-OpenSSH_9.6");
96
        assert_eq!(consumed, buf.len());
97
    }
98
99
    #[test]
100
    fn server_rejects_banner_lines() {
101
        let buf = b"garbage\r\nSSH-2.0-x\r\n";
102
        assert!(matches!(
103
            parse_peer_id(buf, false),
104
            Err(SshError::BadVersion(_))
105
        ));
106
    }
107
108
    #[test]
109
    fn needs_more_bytes_when_incomplete() {
110
        let buf = b"SSH-2.0-OpenSSH_9.6";
111
        assert_eq!(parse_peer_id(buf, true).unwrap(), None);
112
    }
113
114
    #[test]
115
    fn local_id_line_is_crlf_terminated() {
116
        assert_eq!(local_id_line(), b"SSH-2.0-rust_ssh_0.1.0\r\n");
117
    }
118
}
/home/nick/proj/rust/ssh-bench/crates/ssh-transport/src/wire.rs
Line
Count
Source
1
//! Reader/writer for the SSH wire data types defined in RFC 4251 §5
2
//! (`byte`, `boolean`, `uint32`, `uint64`, `string`, `mpint`, `name-list`).
3
//!
4
//! Protocol messages are framed by hand here rather than via `ssh-encoding`'s trait
5
//! machinery so the byte layout of every message — which feeds directly into the
6
//! exchange hash — is explicit and auditable. `ssh-key`/`ssh-encoding` are still used
7
//! for key and signature *blobs*.
8
9
use crate::{Result, SshError};
10
11
/// Cursor over a borrowed byte buffer that decodes SSH primitives.
12
pub struct Reader<'a> {
13
    buf: &'a [u8],
14
    pos: usize,
15
}
16
17
impl<'a> Reader<'a> {
18
843
    pub fn new(buf: &'a [u8]) -> Self {
19
843
        Reader { buf, pos: 0 }
20
843
    }
21
22
    /// Bytes not yet consumed.
23
0
    pub fn remaining(&self) -> &'a [u8] {
24
0
        &self.buf[self.pos..]
25
0
    }
26
27
0
    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
28
0
        self.pos >= self.buf.len()
29
0
    }
30
31
9.56k
    fn take(&mut self, n: usize) -> Result<&'a [u8]> {
32
9.56k
        let end = self
33
9.56k
            .pos
34
9.56k
            .checked_add(n)
35
9.56k
            .ok_or(SshError::Encoding("length overflow"))?;
36
9.56k
        if end > self.buf.len() {
37
441
            return Err(SshError::Encoding("unexpected end of buffer"));
38
9.12k
        }
39
9.12k
        let slice = &self.buf[self.pos..end];
40
9.12k
        self.pos = end;
41
9.12k
        Ok(slice)
42
9.56k
    }
43
44
4.62k
    pub fn u8(&mut self) -> Result<u8> {
45
4.62k
        Ok(self.take(1)?[0])
46
4.62k
    }
47
48
109
    pub fn boolean(&mut self) -> Result<bool> {
49
        // RFC 4251: any non-zero value is interpreted as true.
50
109
        Ok(self.u8()? != 0)
51
109
    }
52
53
2.91k
    pub fn u32(&mut self) -> Result<u32> {
54
2.91k
        let b = self.take(4)?;
55
2.72k
        Ok(u32::from_be_bytes([b[0], b[1], b[2], b[3]]))
56
2.91k
    }
57
58
0
    pub fn u64(&mut self) -> Result<u64> {
59
0
        let b = self.take(8)?;
60
0
        Ok(u64::from_be_bytes([
61
0
            b[0], b[1], b[2], b[3], b[4], b[5], b[6], b[7],
62
0
        ]))
63
0
    }
64
65
    /// A `string`: a `uint32` length followed by that many bytes (returned borrowed).
66
2.21k
    pub fn string(&mut self) -> Result<&'a [u8]> {
67
2.21k
        let len = self.u32()? as usize;
68
2.02k
        self.take(len)
69
2.21k
    }
70
71
    /// A `string` that must be valid UTF-8 (e.g. user names, algorithm names).
72
595
    pub fn utf8(&mut self) -> Result<&'a str> {
73
595
        core::str::from_utf8(self.string()?).map_err(|_| SshError::Encoding("invalid utf-8"))
74
595
    }
75
76
    /// An `mpint`, returned as the raw (signed, two's-complement) bytes on the wire.
77
    /// Callers that want the unsigned magnitude should strip a single leading `0x00`.
78
0
    pub fn mpint(&mut self) -> Result<&'a [u8]> {
79
0
        self.string()
80
0
    }
81
82
    /// A `name-list`: a `string` of comma-separated US-ASCII names.
83
1.61k
    pub fn name_list(&mut self) -> Result<Vec<Box<str>>> {
84
1.61k
        let s = self.string()?;
85
1.52k
        if s.is_empty() {
86
834
            return Ok(Vec::new());
87
690
        }
88
690
        if !s.is_ascii() {
89
26
            return Err(SshError::Encoding("non-ascii name-list"));
90
664
        }
91
        // SAFETY-free: we just checked the bytes are ASCII, hence valid UTF-8.
92
664
        let s = core::str::from_utf8(s).map_err(|_| SshError::Encoding("invalid name-list"))?;
93
664
        Ok(s.split(',').map(Box::from).collect())
94
1.61k
    }
95
}
96
97
/// Appends SSH primitives to an owned byte buffer.
98
#[derive(Default)]
99
pub struct Writer {
100
    buf: Vec<u8>,
101
}
102
103
impl Writer {
104
358

358
        Writer::default()
106
358
    }
107
108
358
    pub fn into_bytes(self) -> Vec<u8> {

358
        self.buf
110
358
    }
111
112
0

0
        &self.buf
114
0
    }
115
116
0
    pub fn len(&self) -> usize {

0
        self.buf.len()
118
0
    }
119
120
0
    pub fn is_empty(&self) -> bool {
121
0
        self.buf.is_empty()
122
0
    }
123
124
353
    pub fn u8(&mut self, v: u8) {
125
353
        self.buf.push(v);
126
353
    }
127
128
353
    pub fn boolean(&mut self, v: bool) {
129
353
        self.buf.push(v as u8);
130
353
    }
131
132
3.89k
    pub fn u32(&mut self, v: u32) {
133
3.89k
        self.buf.extend_from_slice(&v.to_be_bytes());
134
3.89k
    }
135
136
0
    pub fn u64(&mut self, v: u64) {
137
0

138
0
    }
139
140
    /// Raw bytes with no length prefix.
141
353
    pub fn raw(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) {
142
353
        self.buf.extend_from_slice(bytes);
143
353
    }
144
145
    /// A `string`: `uint32` length prefix followed by the bytes.
146
3.54k
    pub fn string(&mut self, bytes: &[u8]) {
147
3.54k
        self.u32(bytes.len() as u32);
148
3.54k
        self.buf.extend_from_slice(bytes);
149
3.54k
    }
150
151
    /// A `name-list` built from individual names.
152
3.53k
    pub fn name_list(&mut self, names: &[&str]) {
153
3.53k
        let joined = names.join(",");
154
3.53k
        self.string(joined.as_bytes());
155
3.53k
    }
156
157
    /// An `mpint` from an unsigned big-endian magnitude: leading zero bytes are
158
    /// trimmed and a single `0x00` is prepended if the top bit would otherwise be set
159
    /// (so the value is never misread as negative). Zero encodes as an empty string.
160
0
    pub fn mpint(&mut self, magnitude: &[u8]) {
161
0

162
0

163
0
            None => self.u32(0), // value is zero

0

0

0
                    self.string(mag);

0
                }
173
            }

        }

0
    }
176
}
177
178
#[cfg(test)]
179
mod tests {
180
    use super::*;
181
182
    #[test]
183
    fn roundtrip_scalars() {
184
        let mut w = Writer::new();
185
        w.u8(0x7f);
186
        w.boolean(true);
187
        w.u32(0xdead_beef);
188
        w.u64(0x0123_4567_89ab_cdef);
189
        let bytes = w.into_bytes();
190
191
        let mut r = Reader::new(&bytes);
192
        assert_eq!(r.u8().unwrap(), 0x7f);
193
        assert!(r.boolean().unwrap());
194
        assert_eq!(r.u32().unwrap(), 0xdead_beef);
195
        assert_eq!(r.u64().unwrap(), 0x0123_4567_89ab_cdef);
196
        assert!(r.is_empty());
197
    }
198
199
    #[test]
200
    fn roundtrip_string_and_name_list() {
201
        let mut w = Writer::new();
202
        w.string(b"hello");
203
        w.name_list(&["curve25519-sha256", "ssh-ed25519"]);
204
        let bytes = w.into_bytes();
205
206
        let mut r = Reader::new(&bytes);
207
        assert_eq!(r.string().unwrap(), b"hello");
208
        assert_eq!(
209
            r.name_list().unwrap(),
210
            vec![
211
                Box::<str>::from("curve25519-sha256"),
212
                Box::<str>::from("ssh-ed25519")
213
            ]
214
        );
215
    }
216
217
    #[test]
218
    fn empty_name_list_is_empty_vec() {
219
        let mut w = Writer::new();
220
        w.name_list(&[]);
221
        let bytes = w.into_bytes();
222
        // An empty name-list is a string of length 0.
223
        assert_eq!(bytes, vec![0, 0, 0, 0]);
224
        let mut r = Reader::new(&bytes);
225
        assert!(r.name_list().unwrap().is_empty());
226
    }
227
228
    #[test]
229
    fn mpint_prepends_zero_when_high_bit_set() {
230
        // 0x80 has its high bit set, so it must be encoded as 00 80.
231
        let mut w = Writer::new();
232
        w.mpint(&[0x80]);
233
        assert_eq!(w.into_bytes(), vec![0, 0, 0, 2, 0x00, 0x80]);
234
    }
235
236
    #[test]
237
    fn mpint_trims_leading_zeros() {
238
        let mut w = Writer::new();
239
        w.mpint(&[0x00, 0x00, 0x09, 0xa3]);
240
        assert_eq!(w.into_bytes(), vec![0, 0, 0, 2, 0x09, 0xa3]);
241
    }
242
243
    #[test]
244
    fn mpint_zero_is_empty() {
245
        let mut w = Writer::new();
246
        w.mpint(&[0x00, 0x00]);
247
        assert_eq!(w.into_bytes(), vec![0, 0, 0, 0]);
248
    }
249
250
    #[test]
251
    fn reader_rejects_truncated_string() {
252
        let buf = [0, 0, 0, 8, 1, 2, 3]; // claims 8 bytes, only 3 present
253
        let mut r = Reader::new(&buf);
254
        assert!(matches!(r.string(), Err(SshError::Encoding(_))));
255
    }
256
}
